authors_text: Yuchen Xiao,Weihao Tan,Christopher Amato
title: Asynchronous Actor-Critic for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 用于多智能体强化学习的异步 Actor-Critic
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2209.10003
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2209.10003
abstract: Synchronizing decisions across multiple agents in realistic settings is problematic since it requires agents to wait for other agents to terminate and communicate about termination reliably. Ideally, agents should learn and execute asynchronously instead. Such asynchronous methods also allow temporally extended actions that can take different amounts of time based on the situation and action executed. Unfortunately, current policy gradient methods are not applicable in asynchronous settings, as they assume that agents synchronously reason about action selection at every time step. To allow asynchronous learning and decision-making, we formulate a set of asynchronous multi-agent actor-critic methods that allow agents to directly optimize asynchronous policies in three standard training paradigms: decentralized learning, centralized learning, and centralized training for decentralized execution. Empirical results (in simulation and hardware) in a variety of realistic domains demonstrate the superiority of our approaches in large multi-agent problems and validate the effectiveness of our algorithms for learning high-quality and asynchronous solutions.
summary: Synchronizing decisions across multiple agents in realistic settings is problematic since it requires agents to wait for other agents to terminate and communicate about termination reliably. Ideally, agents should learn and execute asynchronously instead. Such asynchronous methods also allow temporally extended actions that can take different amounts of time based on the situation and action executed. Unfortunately, current policy gradient methods are not applicable in asynchronous settings, as they assume that agents synchronously reason about action selection at every time step. To allow asynchronous learning and decision-making, we formulate a set of asynchronous multi-agent actor-critic methods that allow agents to directly optimize asynchronous policies in three standard training paradigms: decentralized learning, centralized learning, and centralized training for decentralized execution. Empirical results (in simulation and hardware) in a variety of realistic domains demonstrate the superiority of our approaches in large multi-agent problems and validate the effectiveness of our algorithms for learning high-quality and asynchronous solutions.
abstract_zh: 在现实设置中同步多个代理之间的决策是有问题的，因为它需要代理等待其他代理终止并可靠地传达有关终止的信息。理想情况下，代理应该异步学习和执行。此类异步方法还允许临时延长操作，这些操作可能根据情况和执行的操作而花费不同的时间。不幸的是，当前的策略梯度方法不适用于异步设置，因为它们假设代理在每个时间步骤同步推理动作选择。为了实现异步学习和决策，我们制定了一套异步多智能体行动批评家方法，允许智能体在三种标准训练范式中直接优化异步策略：去中心化学习、集中学习和去中心化执行的集中训练。各种现实领域的经验结果（模拟和硬件）证明了我们的方法在大型多智能体问题中的优越性，并验证了我们的算法用于学习高质量和异步解决方案的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种适用于多智能体强化学习的异步Actor-Critic方法，解决异步决策中的同步问题。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中多智能体同步决策需等待通信，导致效率低下，而现有策略梯度方法无法处理异步操作。
AI.method: 设计异步多智能体Actor-Critic框架，支持分散学习、集中学习及集中训练分散执行三种范式，直接优化异步策略。
AI.result: 在仿真与硬件实验中，该方法在复杂多智能体任务中表现优于现有算法，验证了异步策略的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 异步Actor-Critic方法能有效提升多智能体系统的决策效率与解决方案质量，适用于大规模实际场景。

authors_text: Yiwei Wang,Bryan Hooi,Yozen Liu,Tong Zhao,Zhichun Guo,Neil Shah
title: Flashlight: Scalable Link Prediction with Effective Decoders
title_zh: Flashlight：具有有效解码器的可扩展链路预测
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.02936 by other authors
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.02936 by other authors
abstract: Link prediction (LP) has been recognized as an important task in graph learning with its broad practical applications. A typical application of LP is to retrieve the top scoring neighbors for a given source node, such as the friend recommendation. These services desire the high inference scalability to find the top scoring neighbors from many candidate nodes at low latencies. There are two popular decoders that the recent LP models mainly use to compute the edge scores from node embeddings: the HadamardMLP and Dot Product decoders. After theoretical and empirical analysis, we find that the HadamardMLP decoders are generally more effective for LP. However, HadamardMLP lacks the scalability for retrieving top scoring neighbors on large graphs, since to the best of our knowledge, there does not exist an algorithm to retrieve the top scoring neighbors for HadamardMLP decoders in sublinear complexity. To make HadamardMLP scalable, we propose the Flashlight algorithm to accelerate the top scoring neighbor retrievals for HadamardMLP: a sublinear algorithm that progressively applies approximate maximum inner product search (MIPS) techniques with adaptively adjusted query embeddings. Empirical results show that Flashlight improves the inference speed of LP by more than 100 times on the large OGBL-CITATION2 dataset without sacrificing effectiveness. Our work paves the way for large-scale LP applications with the effective HadamardMLP decoders by greatly accelerating their inference.
summary: Link prediction (LP) has been recognized as an important task in graph learning with its broad practical applications. A typical application of LP is to retrieve the top scoring neighbors for a given source node, such as the friend recommendation. These services desire the high inference scalability to find the top scoring neighbors from many candidate nodes at low latencies. There are two popular decoders that the recent LP models mainly use to compute the edge scores from node embeddings: the HadamardMLP and Dot Product decoders. After theoretical and empirical analysis, we find that the HadamardMLP decoders are generally more effective for LP. However, HadamardMLP lacks the scalability for retrieving top scoring neighbors on large graphs, since to the best of our knowledge, there does not exist an algorithm to retrieve the top scoring neighbors for HadamardMLP decoders in sublinear complexity. To make HadamardMLP scalable, we propose the Flashlight algorithm to accelerate the top scoring neighbor retrievals for HadamardMLP: a sublinear algorithm that progressively applies approximate maximum inner product search (MIPS) techniques with adaptively adjusted query embeddings. Empirical results show that Flashlight improves the inference speed of LP by more than 100 times on the large OGBL-CITATION2 dataset without sacrificing effectiveness. Our work paves the way for large-scale LP applications with the effective HadamardMLP decoders by greatly accelerating their inference.
abstract_zh: 链接预测（LP）因其广泛的实际应用而被认为是图学习中的一项重要任务。 LP 的一个典型应用是检索给定源节点的得分最高的邻居，例如朋友推荐。这些服务需要高推理可扩展性，以便以低延迟从许多候选节点中找到得分最高的邻居。最近的 LP 模型主要使用两种流行的解码器来计算节点嵌入的边缘分数：HadamardMLP 和点积解码器。经过理论和实证分析，我们发现 HadamardMLP 解码器通常对于 LP 更有效。然而，HadamardMLP 缺乏在大图上检索得分最高的邻居的可扩展性，因为据我们所知，不存在一种算法可以在亚线性复杂度下检索 HadamardMLP 解码器的得分最高的邻居。为了使 HadamardMLP 可扩展，我们提出了 Flashlight 算法来加速 HadamardMLP 的最高得分邻居检索：一种次线性算法，逐步应用近似最大内积搜索 (MIPS) 技术和自适应调整的查询嵌入。实证结果表明，Flashlight 在大型 OGBL-CITATION2 数据集上将 LP 的推理速度提高了 100 倍以上，且不牺牲有效性。我们的工作通过大幅加速推理，为使用有效的 HadamardMLP 解码器的大规模 LP 应用铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Flashlight的算法，用于加速基于HadamardMLP解码器的链接预测任务中的高得分邻居检索。
AI.motivation: 尽管HadamardMLP解码器在链接预测中效果更好，但其在大规模图中缺乏可扩展性，现有方法无法以次线性复杂度高效检索高得分邻居。
AI.method: 设计Flashlight算法，通过渐进式应用近似最大内积搜索（MIPS）技术，并结合自适应调整的查询嵌入，实现对HadamardMLP的高效邻居检索。
AI.result: 在OGBL-CITATION2数据集上，Flashlight将链接预测的推理速度提升超过100倍，同时保持效果不变。
AI.conclusion: Flashlight通过显著加速HadamardMLP的推理过程，为大规模链接预测应用铺平了道路。

authors_text: Elodie Germani,Elisa Fromont,Camille Maumet
title: On the benefits of self-taught learning for brain decoding
title_zh: 论自学学习对大脑解码的好处
abstract: Context. We study the benefits of using a large public neuroimaging database composed of fMRI statistic maps, in a self-taught learning framework, for improving brain decoding on new tasks. First, we leverage the NeuroVault database to train, on a selection of relevant statistic maps, a convolutional autoencoder to reconstruct these maps. Then, we use this trained encoder to initialize a supervised convolutional neural network to classify tasks or cognitive processes of unseen statistic maps from large collections of the NeuroVault database. Results. We show that such a self-taught learning process always improves the performance of the classifiers but the magnitude of the benefits strongly depends on the number of samples available both for pre-training and finetuning the models and on the complexity of the targeted downstream task. Conclusion. The pre-trained model improves the classification performance and displays more generalizable features, less sensitive to individual differences.
summary: Context. We study the benefits of using a large public neuroimaging database composed of fMRI statistic maps, in a self-taught learning framework, for improving brain decoding on new tasks. First, we leverage the NeuroVault database to train, on a selection of relevant statistic maps, a convolutional autoencoder to reconstruct these maps. Then, we use this trained encoder to initialize a supervised convolutional neural network to classify tasks or cognitive processes of unseen statistic maps from large collections of the NeuroVault database. Results. We show that such a self-taught learning process always improves the performance of the classifiers but the magnitude of the benefits strongly depends on the number of samples available both for pre-training and finetuning the models and on the complexity of the targeted downstream task. Conclusion. The pre-trained model improves the classification performance and displays more generalizable features, less sensitive to individual differences.
abstract_zh: 语境。我们研究了在自学学习框架中使用由功能磁共振成像统计图组成的大型公共神经影像数据库对于改善新任务的大脑解码的好处。首先，我们利用 NeuroVault 数据库在精选的相关统计图上训练卷积自动编码器来重建这些图。然后，我们使用这个经过训练的编码器来初始化一个监督卷积神经网络，以对来自 NeuroVault 数据库的大量集合中未见过的统计图的任务或认知过程进行分类。结果。我们表明，这种自学学习过程总是可以提高分类器的性能，但好处的大小在很大程度上取决于可用于预训练和微调模型的样本数量以及目标下游任务的复杂性。结论。预训练的模型提高了分类性能并显示出更通用的特征，对个体差异不太敏感。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在自我教学学习框架下，利用大型公开神经影像数据库改进脑解码任务的效果。
AI.motivation: 通过利用公开的fMRI统计图数据预训练模型，解决新任务中样本量不足和任务复杂性带来的脑解码性能瓶颈。
AI.method: 基于NeuroVault数据库选取相关统计图，训练卷积自编码器进行重构，再用其编码器初始化监督卷积神经网络对未见任务进行分类。
AI.result: 自我教学学习显著提升分类性能，但收益幅度受预训练/微调样本量及下游任务复杂度影响显著。
AI.conclusion: 预训练模型能提升分类准确率并生成更具泛化性的特征，降低个体差异敏感性。

search_text: On the benefits of self-taught learning for brain decoding 论自学学习对大脑解码的好处 Context. We study the benefits of using a large public neuroimaging database composed of fMRI statistic maps, in a self-taught learning framework, for improving brain decoding on new tasks. First, we leverage the NeuroVault database to train, on a selection of relevant statistic maps, a convolutional autoencoder to reconstruct these maps. Then, we use this trained encoder to initialize a supervised convolutional neural network to classify tasks or cognitive processes of unseen statistic maps from large collections of the NeuroVault database. Results. We show that such a self-taught learning process always improves the performance of the classifiers but the magnitude of the benefits strongly depends on the number of samples available both for pre-training and finetuning the models and on the complexity of the targeted downstream task. Conclusion. The pre-trained model improves the classification performance and displays more generalizable features, less sensitive to individual differences. 语境。我们研究了在自学学习框架中使用由功能磁共振成像统计图组成的大型公共神经影像数据库对于改善新任务的大脑解码的好处。首先，我们利用 NeuroVault 数据库在精选的相关统计图上训练卷积自动编码器来重建这些图。然后，我们使用这个经过训练的编码器来初始化一个监督卷积神经网络，以对来自 NeuroVault 数据库的大量集合中未见过的统计图的任务或认知过程进行分类。结果。我们表明，这种自学学习过程总是可以提高分类器的性能，但好处的大小在很大程度上取决于可用于预训练和微调模型的样本数量以及目标下游任务的复杂性。结论。预训练的模型提高了分类性能并显示出更通用的特征，对个体差异不太敏感。 本文研究了在自我教学学习框架下，利用大型公开神经影像数据库改进脑解码任务的效果。 Elodie Germani,Elisa Fromont,Camille Maumet cs.NE,cs.AI

authors_text: Jiaqi Gu,Zhengqi Gao,Chenghao Feng,Hanqing Zhu,Ray T. Chen,Duane S. Boning,David Z. Pan
title: NeurOLight: A Physics-Agnostic Neural Operator Enabling Parametric Photonic Device Simulation
title_zh: NeurOLight：一种与物理无关的神经算子，支持参数光子器件模拟
comment: 13 pages. Accepted to NeurIPS 2022
comments: 13 pages. Accepted to NeurIPS 2022
abstract: Optical computing is an emerging technology for next-generation efficient artificial intelligence (AI) due to its ultra-high speed and efficiency. Electromagnetic field simulation is critical to the design, optimization, and validation of photonic devices and circuits. However, costly numerical simulation significantly hinders the scalability and turn-around time in the photonic circuit design loop. Recently, physics-informed neural networks have been proposed to predict the optical field solution of a single instance of a partial differential equation (PDE) with predefined parameters. Their complicated PDE formulation and lack of efficient parametrization mechanisms limit their flexibility and generalization in practical simulation scenarios. In this work, for the first time, a physics-agnostic neural operator-based framework, dubbed NeurOLight, is proposed to learn a family of frequency-domain Maxwell PDEs for ultra-fast parametric photonic device simulation. We balance the efficiency and generalization of NeurOLight via several novel techniques. Specifically, we discretize different devices into a unified domain, represent parametric PDEs with a compact wave prior, and encode the incident light via masked source modeling. We design our model with parameter-efficient cross-shaped NeurOLight blocks and adopt superposition-based augmentation for data-efficient learning. With these synergistic approaches, NeurOLight generalizes to a large space of unseen simulation settings, demonstrates 2-orders-of-magnitude faster simulation speed than numerical solvers, and outperforms prior neural network models by ~54% lower prediction error with ~44% fewer parameters. Our code is available at https://github.com/JeremieMelo/NeurOLight.

Electromagnetic field simulation is critical to the design, optimization, and validation of photonic devices and circuits. However, costly numerical simulation significantly hinders the scalability and turn-around time in the photonic circuit design loop. Recently, physics-informed neural networks have been proposed to predict the optical field solution of a single instance of a partial differential equation (PDE) with predefined parameters. Their complicated PDE formulation and lack of efficient parametrization mechanisms limit their flexibility and generalization in practical simulation scenarios. In this work, for the first time, a physics-agnostic neural operator-based framework, dubbed NeurOLight, is proposed to learn a family of frequency-domain Maxwell PDEs for ultra-fast parametric photonic device simulation. We balance the efficiency and generalization of NeurOLight via several novel techniques. Specifically, we discretize different devices into a unified domain, represent parametric PDEs with a compact wave prior, and encode the incident light via masked source modeling. We design our model with parameter-efficient cross-shaped NeurOLight blocks and adopt superposition-based augmentation for data-efficient learning. With these synergistic approaches, NeurOLight generalizes to a large space of unseen simulation settings, demonstrates 2-orders-of-magnitude faster simulation speed than numerical solvers, and outperforms prior neural network models by ~54% lower prediction error with ~44% fewer parameters. Our code is available at https://github.com/JeremieMelo/NeurOLight.
abstract_zh: 光计算因其超高的速度和效率而成为下一代高效人工智能（AI）的新兴技术。电磁场仿真对于光子器件和电路的设计、优化和验证至关重要。然而，昂贵的数值模拟极大地阻碍了光子电路设计循环中的可扩展性和周转时间。最近，人们提出了基于物理的神经网络来预测具有预定义参数的偏微分方程（PDE）的单个实例的光场解。它们复杂的偏微分方程公式和缺乏有效的参数化机制限制了它们在实际模拟场景中的灵活性和泛化性。在这项工作中，首次提出了一种与物理无关的神经算子框架，称为 NeurOLight，用于学习一系列频域麦克斯韦偏微分方程，用于超快参数光子器件模拟。我们通过几种新技术平衡了 NeurOLight 的效率和泛化性。具体来说，我们将不同的设备离散到统一的域中，用紧凑波先验表示参数偏微分方程，并通过掩模源建模对入射光进行编码。我们使用参数高效的十字形 NeurOLight 模块设计模型，并采用基于叠加的增强来实现数据高效的学习。通过这些协同方法，NeurOLight 可以推广到大量未见过的模拟设置，其模拟速度比数值求解器快 2 个数量级，并且比之前的神经网络模型的预测误差降低约 54%，参数减少约 44%。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/JeremieMelo/NeurOLight 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为NeurOLight的物理无关神经算子框架，用于实现参数化光子器件的超快速模拟。
AI.motivation: 传统数值模拟方法在光子器件设计中存在计算成本高、效率低的问题，而现有物理感知神经网络在灵活性和泛化性上存在局限。
AI.method: 通过统一域离散化、紧凑波先验参数化、掩码源建模等技术，结合参数高效的十字形NeurOLight模块和基于叠加的增强学习方法。
AI.result: 相比数值求解器快2个数量级，预测误差降低54%，参数量减少44%，可泛化至多种未见过的仿真场景。
AI.conclusion: NeurOLight为光子器件参数化仿真提供了高效且通用的解决方案，显著优于现有神经网络模型。

authors_text: Shijing Si,Jianzong Wang,Xulong Zhang,Xiaoyang Qu,Ning Cheng,Jing Xiao
title: Boosting Star-GANs for Voice Conversion with Contrastive Discriminator
title_zh: 使用对比鉴别器增强 Star-GAN 进行语音转换
comment: 12 pages, 3 figures, Accepted by ICONIP 2022
comments: 12 pages, 3 figures, Accepted by ICONIP 2022
abstract: Nonparallel multi-domain voice conversion methods such as the StarGAN-VCs have been widely applied in many scenarios. However, the training of these models usually poses a challenge due to their complicated adversarial network architectures. To address this, in this work we leverage the state-of-the-art contrastive learning techniques and incorporate an efficient Siamese network structure into the StarGAN discriminator. Our method is called SimSiam-StarGAN-VC and it boosts the training stability and effectively prevents the discriminator overfitting issue in the training process. We conduct experiments on the Voice Conversion Challenge (VCC 2018) dataset, plus a user study to validate the performance of our framework. Our experimental results show that SimSiam-StarGAN-VC significantly outperforms existing StarGAN-VC methods in terms of both the objective and subjective metrics.
summary: Nonparallel multi-domain voice conversion methods such as the StarGAN-VCs have been widely applied in many scenarios. However, the training of these models usually poses a challenge due to their complicated adversarial network architectures. To address this, in this work we leverage the state-of-the-art contrastive learning techniques and incorporate an efficient Siamese network structure into the StarGAN discriminator. Our method is called SimSiam-StarGAN-VC and it boosts the training stability and effectively prevents the discriminator overfitting issue in the training process. We conduct experiments on the Voice Conversion Challenge (VCC 2018) dataset, plus a user study to validate the performance of our framework. Our experimental results show that SimSiam-StarGAN-VC significantly outperforms existing StarGAN-VC methods in terms of both the objective and subjective metrics.
abstract_zh: StarGAN-VC等非并行多域语音转换方法已在许多场景中得到广泛应用。然而，由于其复杂的对抗网络架构，这些模型的训练通常会带来挑战。为了解决这个问题，在这项工作中，我们利用最先进的对比学习技术，并将高效的 Siamese 网络结构纳入 StarGAN 判别器中。我们的方法称为 SimSiam-StarGAN-VC，它提高了训练稳定性，并有效防止训练过程中的判别器过拟合问题。我们在语音转换挑战赛 (VCC 2018) 数据集上进行实验，并进行用户研究来验证我们框架的性能。我们的实验结果表明，SimSiam-StarGAN-VC 在客观和主观指标方面均明显优于现有的 StarGAN-VC 方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SimSiam-StarGAN-VC方法，通过引入对比学习和Siamese网络结构改进StarGAN-VC模型，解决语音转换中的训练稳定性与判别器过拟合问题。
AI.motivation: 现有StarGAN-VC模型因对抗网络结构复杂，存在训练不稳定和判别器过拟合问题，影响语音转换效果。
AI.method: 结合对比学习技术与高效Siamese网络结构，改进StarGAN判别器设计，提出SimSiam-StarGAN-VC框架。
AI.result: 在VCC 2018数据集上实验表明，该方法在客观指标和主观评价中均显著优于现有StarGAN-VC方法。
AI.conclusion: SimSiam-StarGAN-VC通过结构改进有效提升了模型训练稳定性与语音转换质量，验证了对比学习在对抗网络中的应用价值。

We conduct experiments on the Voice Conversion Challenge (VCC 2018) dataset, plus a user study to validate the performance of our framework. Our experimental results show that SimSiam-StarGAN-VC significantly outperforms existing StarGAN-VC methods in terms of both the objective and subjective metrics. StarGAN-VC等非并行多域语音转换方法已在许多场景中得到广泛应用。然而，由于其复杂的对抗网络架构，这些模型的训练通常会带来挑战。为了解决这个问题，在这项工作中，我们利用最先进的对比学习技术，并将高效的 Siamese 网络结构纳入 StarGAN 判别器中。我们的方法称为 SimSiam-StarGAN-VC，它提高了训练稳定性，并有效防止训练过程中的判别器过拟合问题。我们在语音转换挑战赛 (VCC 2018) 数据集上进行实验，并进行用户研究来验证我们框架的性能。我们的实验结果表明，SimSiam-StarGAN-VC 在客观和主观指标方面均明显优于现有的 StarGAN-VC 方法。 本文提出SimSiam-StarGAN-VC方法，通过引入对比学习和Siamese网络结构改进StarGAN-VC模型，解决语音转换中的训练稳定性与判别器过拟合问题。 Shijing Si,Jianzong Wang,Xulong Zhang,Xiaoyang Qu,Ning Cheng,Jing Xiao eess.AS,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.SD

authors_text: Yue Tan,Guodong Long,Jie Ma,Lu Liu,Tianyi Zhou,Jing Jiang
title: Federated Learning from Pre-Trained Models: A Contrastive Learning Approach
title_zh: 来自预训练模型的联邦学习：一种对比学习方法
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a machine learning paradigm that allows decentralized clients to learn collaboratively without sharing their private data. However, excessive computation and communication demands pose challenges to current FL frameworks, especially when training large-scale models. To prevent these issues from hindering the deployment of FL systems, we propose a lightweight framework where clients jointly learn to fuse the representations generated by multiple fixed pre-trained models rather than training a large-scale model from scratch. This leads us to a more practical FL problem by considering how to capture more client-specific and class-relevant information from the pre-trained models and jointly improve each client's ability to exploit those off-the-shelf models. In this work, we design a Federated Prototype-wise Contrastive Learning (FedPCL) approach which shares knowledge across clients through their class prototypes and builds client-specific representations in a prototype-wise contrastive manner. Sharing prototypes rather than learnable model parameters allows each client to fuse the representations in a personalized way while keeping the shared knowledge in a compact form for efficient communication. We perform a thorough evaluation of the proposed FedPCL in the lightweight framework, measuring and visualizing its ability to fuse various pre-trained models on popular FL datasets.
summary: Federated Learning (FL) is a machine learning paradigm that allows decentralized clients to learn collaboratively without sharing their private data. However, excessive computation and communication demands pose challenges to current FL frameworks, especially when training large-scale models. To prevent these issues from hindering the deployment of FL systems, we propose a lightweight framework where clients jointly learn to fuse the representations generated by multiple fixed pre-trained models rather than training a large-scale model from scratch. This leads us to a more practical FL problem by considering how to capture more client-specific and class-relevant information from the pre-trained models and jointly improve each client's ability to exploit those off-the-shelf models. In this work, we design a Federated Prototype-wise Contrastive Learning (FedPCL) approach which shares knowledge across clients through their class prototypes and builds client-specific representations in a prototype-wise contrastive manner. Sharing prototypes rather than learnable model parameters allows each client to fuse the representations in a personalized way while keeping the shared knowledge in a compact form for efficient communication. We perform a thorough evaluation of the proposed FedPCL in the lightweight framework, measuring and visualizing its ability to fuse various pre-trained models on popular FL datasets.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习 (FL) 是一种机器学习范例，允许分散的客户在不共享其私人数据的情况下进行协作学习。然而，过多的计算和通信需求给当前的 FL 框架带来了挑战，特别是在训练大规模模型时。为了防止这些问题阻碍 FL 系统的部署，我们提出了一个轻量级框架，客户可以共同学习融合多个固定预训练模型生成的表示，而不是从头开始训练大规模模型。这让我们遇到了一个更实际的 FL 问题，即考虑如何从预训练模型中捕获更多特定于客户和类别相关的信息，并共同提高每个客户利用这些现成模型的能力。在这项工作中，我们设计了一种联合原型对比学习（FedPCL）方法，该方法通过客户的类原型在客户之间共享知识，并以原型对比方式构建特定于客户的表示。共享原型而不是可学习的模型参数允许每个客户端以个性化的方式融合表示，同时以紧凑的形式保持共享知识以进行有效的通信。我们在轻量级框架中对提出的 FedPCL 进行了全面评估，测量并可视化其在流行 FL 数据集上融合各种预训练模型的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级联邦学习框架，通过预训练模型和对比学习方法解决传统联邦学习中的计算与通信开销问题。
AI.motivation: 当前联邦学习框架在训练大规模模型时面临计算和通信资源消耗过大的挑战，需寻找更高效且实用的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计联邦原型级对比学习（FedPCL）方法，通过共享类原型而非可学习参数，在预训练模型生成的表示中构建客户端特定的对比表示。
AI.result: 在主流联邦学习数据集上验证了FedPCL框架的有效性，证明其能高效融合多种预训练模型并保持通信效率。
AI.conclusion: FedPCL通过原型共享机制实现了对预训练模型的个性化利用，在降低资源消耗的同时提升了联邦学习的实用性。

authors_text: Dangxing Chen,Weicheng Ye
title: Generalized Groves of Neural Additive Models: Pursuing transparent and accurate machine learning models in finance
title_zh: 广义神经加性模型树丛：追求金融领域透明且准确的机器学习模型
abstract: While machine learning methods have significantly improved model performance over traditional methods, their black-box structure makes it difficult for researchers to interpret results. For highly regulated financial industries, model transparency is equally important to accuracy. Without understanding how models work, even highly accurate machine learning methods are unlikely to be accepted. We address this issue by introducing a novel class of transparent machine learning models known as generalized groves of neural additive models. The generalized groves of neural additive models separate features into three categories: linear features, individual nonlinear features, and interacted nonlinear features. Additionally, interactions in the last category are only local. A stepwise selection algorithm distinguishes the linear and nonlinear components, and interacted groups are carefully verified by applying additive separation criteria. Through some empirical examples in finance, we demonstrate that generalized grove of neural additive models exhibit high accuracy and transparency with predominantly linear terms and only sparse nonlinear ones.
summary: While machine learning methods have significantly improved model performance over traditional methods, their black-box structure makes it difficult for researchers to interpret results. For highly regulated financial industries, model transparency is equally important to accuracy. Without understanding how models work, even highly accurate machine learning methods are unlikely to be accepted. We address this issue by introducing a novel class of transparent machine learning models known as generalized groves of neural additive models. The generalized groves of neural additive models separate features into three categories: linear features, individual nonlinear features, and interacted nonlinear features. Additionally, interactions in the last category are only local. A stepwise selection algorithm distinguishes the linear and nonlinear components, and interacted groups are carefully verified by applying additive separation criteria. Through some empirical examples in finance, we demonstrate that generalized grove of neural additive models exhibit high accuracy and transparency with predominantly linear terms and only sparse nonlinear ones.
abstract_zh: 虽然机器学习方法比传统方法显着提高了模型性能，但其黑盒结构使研究人员难以解释结果。对于高度监管的金融行业，模型透明度与准确性同样重要。如果不了解模型的工作原理，即使是高度准确的机器学习方法也不太可能被接受。我们通过引入一类新型透明机器学习模型（称为广义神经加性模型树林）来解决这个问题。神经加性模型的广义树林将特征分为三类：线性特征、单独的非线性特征和交互的非线性特征。此外，最后一类中的交互只是本地的。逐步选择算法区分线性和非线性分量，并通过应用加性分离标准仔细验证相互作用的组。通过金融领域的一些实证例子，我们证明了广义的神经加性模型树林表现出高精度和透明度，主要是线性项，只有稀疏的非线性项。

AI.tldr: 提出一种新型透明且高精度的机器学习模型，用于金融领域的应用，解决模型可解释性与准确性之间的矛盾。
AI.motivation: 传统机器学习模型的黑箱特性阻碍其在金融等高监管行业的应用，需兼顾模型准确性与透明度。
AI.method: 通过构建广义神经加法模型的groves结构，将特征分为线性、非线性及局部交互非线性三类，并采用逐步选择算法与可加分离准则进行模型构建与验证。
AI.result: 在金融领域的实证研究表明，该模型在保持高预测精度的同时，实现了模型结构的高透明度，且非线性组件稀疏。
AI.conclusion: 广义神经加法模型的groves结构有效平衡了模型的透明性与预测精度，为金融领域提供了可解释且高效的机器学习解决方案。

authors_text: Haibin Zhou,Tong Wei,Zichuan Lin,junyou li,Junliang Xing,Yuanchun Shi,Li Shen,Chao Yu,Deheng Ye
title: Revisiting Discrete Soft Actor-Critic
title_zh: 重温离散的软演员评论家
comment: Accepted by Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)
comments: Accepted by Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)
abstract: We study the adaption of Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), which is considered as a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, from continuous action space to discrete action space. We revisit vanilla discrete SAC and provide an in-depth understanding of its Q value underestimation and performance instability issues when applied to discrete settings. We thereby propose Stable Discrete SAC (SDSAC), an algorithm that leverages entropy-penalty and double average Q-learning with Q-clip to address these issues. Extensive experiments on typical benchmarks with discrete action space, including Atari games and a large-scale MOBA game, show the efficacy of our proposed method. Our code is at: https://github.com/coldsummerday/SD-SAC.git.
summary: We study the adaption of Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), which is considered as a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, from continuous action space to discrete action space. We revisit vanilla discrete SAC and provide an in-depth understanding of its Q value underestimation and performance instability issues when applied to discrete settings. We thereby propose Stable Discrete SAC (SDSAC), an algorithm that leverages entropy-penalty and double average Q-learning with Q-clip to address these issues. Extensive experiments on typical benchmarks with discrete action space, including Atari games and a large-scale MOBA game, show the efficacy of our proposed method. Our code is at: https://github.com/coldsummerday/SD-SAC.git.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了 Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) 的适应，它被认为是最先进的强化学习 (RL) 算法，从连续动作空间到离散动作空间。我们重新审视普通离散 SAC，并深入了解其应用于离散设置时的 Q 值低估和性能不稳定问题。因此，我们提出了稳定离散 SAC (SDSAC)，这是一种利用熵惩罚和带有 Q-clip 的双平均 Q 学习来解决这些问题的算法。对具有离散动作空间的典型基准（包括 Atari 游戏和大型 MOBA 游戏）进行的大量实验表明了我们提出的方法的有效性。我们的代码位于：https://github.com/coldsummerday/SD-SAC.git。

AI.tldr: 本文研究将Soft Actor-Critic算法从连续动作空间扩展到离散动作空间，并提出改进方法解决其性能问题。
AI.motivation: 现有离散SAC存在Q值低估和性能不稳定问题，需通过改进算法提升其在离散环境中的表现。
AI.method: 提出Stable Discrete SAC（SDSAC），结合熵惩罚、双平均Q学习与Q-clip技术优化算法稳定性。
AI.result: 在Atari游戏和大规模MOBA游戏等离散动作基准测试中验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: SDSAC成功解决了离散SAC的核心问题，实验结果验证了其优越性。

search_text: Revisiting Discrete Soft Actor-Critic 重温离散的软演员评论家 We study the adaption of Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), which is considered as a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, from continuous action space to discrete action space. We revisit vanilla discrete SAC and provide an in-depth understanding of its Q value underestimation and performance instability issues when applied to discrete settings. We thereby propose Stable Discrete SAC (SDSAC), an algorithm that leverages entropy-penalty and double average Q-learning with Q-clip to address these issues. Extensive experiments on typical benchmarks with discrete action space, including Atari games and a large-scale MOBA game, show the efficacy of our proposed method. Our code is at: https://github.com/coldsummerday/SD-SAC.git. 我们研究了 Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) 的适应，它被认为是最先进的强化学习 (RL) 算法，从连续动作空间到离散动作空间。我们重新审视普通离散 SAC，并深入了解其应用于离散设置时的 Q 值低估和性能不稳定问题。因此，我们提出了稳定离散 SAC (SDSAC)，这是一种利用熵惩罚和带有 Q-clip 的双平均 Q 学习来解决这些问题的算法。对具有离散动作空间的典型基准（包括 Atari 游戏和大型 MOBA 游戏）进行的大量实验表明了我们提出的方法的有效性。我们的代码位于：https://github.com/coldsummerday/SD-SAC.git。 本文研究将Soft Actor-Critic算法从连续动作空间扩展到离散动作空间，并提出改进方法解决其性能问题。 Haibin Zhou,Tong Wei,Zichuan Lin,junyou li,Junliang Xing,Yuanchun Shi,Li Shen,Chao Yu,Deheng Ye cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Shuting Kang,Heng Guo,Lijun Zhang,Guangzhen Liu,Yunzhi Xue,Yanjun Wu
title: ECSAS: Exploring Critical Scenarios from Action Sequence in Autonomous Driving
title_zh: ECSAS：从自动驾驶的行动序列中探索关键场景
abstract: Critical scenario generation requires the ability of sampling critical combinations from the infinite parameter space in the logic scenario. Existing solutions aim to explore the correlation of action parameters in the initial scenario rather than action sequences. How to model action sequences so that one can further consider the effects of different action parameters in the scenario is the bottleneck of the problem. In this paper, we attack the problem by proposing the ECSAS framework. Specifically, we first propose a description language, BTScenario, allowing us to model action sequences of the scenarios. We then use reinforcement learning to search for combinations of critical action parameters. To increase efficiency, we further propose several optimizations, including action masking and replay buffer. We have implemented ECSAS, and experimental results show that it is more efficient than native approaches such as random and combination testing in various nontrivial scenarios.
summary: Critical scenario generation requires the ability of sampling critical combinations from the infinite parameter space in the logic scenario. Existing solutions aim to explore the correlation of action parameters in the initial scenario rather than action sequences. How to model action sequences so that one can further consider the effects of different action parameters in the scenario is the bottleneck of the problem. In this paper, we attack the problem by proposing the ECSAS framework. Specifically, we first propose a description language, BTScenario, allowing us to model action sequences of the scenarios. We then use reinforcement learning to search for combinations of critical action parameters. To increase efficiency, we further propose several optimizations, including action masking and replay buffer. We have implemented ECSAS, and experimental results show that it is more efficient than native approaches such as random and combination testing in various nontrivial scenarios.
abstract_zh: 关键场景生成需要能够从逻辑场景中的无限参数空间中采样关键组合。现有的解决方案旨在探索初始场景中动作参数的相关性，而不是动作序列。如何对动作序列进行建模，以便进一步考虑场景中不同动作参数的影响是问题的瓶颈。在本文中，我们通过提出 ECSAS 框架来解决这个问题。具体来说，我们首先提出了一种描述语言 BTScenario，允许我们对场景的动作序列进行建模。然后，我们使用强化学习来搜索关键动作参数的组合。为了提高效率，我们进一步提出了一些优化，包括动作屏蔽和重播缓冲区。我们已经实现了 ECSAS，实验结果表明，它比各种非平凡场景中的随机测试和组合测试等原生方法更有效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ECSAS框架，通过建模动作序列并结合强化学习优化，解决自动驾驶中关键场景生成效率低的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效探索动作序列中参数组合的影响，导致关键场景生成效率低下且覆盖不足。
AI.method: 设计BTScenario描述语言建模动作序列，采用强化学习搜索关键参数组合，并引入动作掩码和回放缓冲区优化算法效率。
AI.result: 实验表明ECSAS在多种复杂场景下显著优于随机测试和组合测试方法，生成关键场景的效率更高。
AI.conclusion: ECSAS通过序列建模和强化学习优化，有效提升了自动驾驶关键场景生成的效率和覆盖性，为安全测试提供新思路。

authors_text: Safa C. Medin,Amir Weiss,Frédo Durand,William T. Freeman,Gregory W. Wornell
title: Can Shadows Reveal Biometric Information?
title_zh: 阴影可以揭示生物识别信息吗？
abstract: We study the problem of extracting biometric information of individuals by looking at shadows of objects cast on diffuse surfaces. We show that the biometric information leakage from shadows can be sufficient for reliable identity inference under representative scenarios via a maximum likelihood analysis. We then develop a learning-based method that demonstrates this phenomenon in real settings, exploiting the subtle cues in the shadows that are the source of the leakage without requiring any labeled real data. In particular, our approach relies on building synthetic scenes composed of 3D face models obtained from a single photograph of each identity. We transfer what we learn from the synthetic data to the real data using domain adaptation in a completely unsupervised way. Our model is able to generalize well to the real domain and is robust to several variations in the scenes. We report high classification accuracies in an identity classification task that takes place in a scene with unknown geometry and occluding objects.
summary: We study the problem of extracting biometric information of individuals by looking at shadows of objects cast on diffuse surfaces. We show that the biometric information leakage from shadows can be sufficient for reliable identity inference under representative scenarios via a maximum likelihood analysis. We then develop a learning-based method that demonstrates this phenomenon in real settings, exploiting the subtle cues in the shadows that are the source of the leakage without requiring any labeled real data. In particular, our approach relies on building synthetic scenes composed of 3D face models obtained from a single photograph of each identity. We transfer what we learn from the synthetic data to the real data using domain adaptation in a completely unsupervised way. Our model is able to generalize well to the real domain and is robust to several variations in the scenes. We report high classification accuracies in an identity classification task that takes place in a scene with unknown geometry and occluding objects.
abstract_zh: 我们研究通过观察物体投射在漫射表面上的阴影来提取个体生物特征信息的问题。我们表明，通过最大似然分析，阴影中的生物识别信息泄漏足以在代表性场景下进行可靠的身份推断。然后，我们开发了一种基于学习的方法，在真实环境中演示这种现象，利用阴影中的微妙线索（泄漏的来源），而不需要任何标记的真实数据。特别是，我们的方法依赖于构建由从每个身份的单张照片获得的 3D 面部模型组成的合成场景。我们以完全无监督的方式使用域适应将从合成数据中学到的知识转移到真实数据中。我们的模型能够很好地推广到真实领域，并且对场景中的多种变化具有鲁棒性。我们报告了在具有未知几何形状和遮挡对象的场景中进行的身份分类任务中的高分类精度。

AI.tldr: 研究阴影是否能泄露生物特征信息，并开发方法验证该现象
AI.motivation: 阴影可能泄露生物信息，导致身份被推断，需研究其可行性及方法
AI.method: 构建基于3D人脸模型的合成场景，利用领域自适应迁移至真实数据，无需标记数据
AI.result: 模型在真实场景中表现良好，对场景变化鲁棒，身份分类准确率高
AI.conclusion: 阴影可泄露生物特征信息，所提方法能有效验证此现象

authors_text: Wenhao Tang,Sheng Huang,Xiaoxian Zhang,Luwen Huangfu
title: PicT: A Slim Weakly Supervised Vision Transformer for Pavement Distress Classification
title_zh: PicT：用于路面病害分类的细长弱监督视觉转换器
comment: ACM Multimedia 2022 paper, 9 pages 7 figures
comments: ACM Multimedia 2022 paper, 9 pages 7 figures
abstract: Automatic pavement distress classification facilitates improving the efficiency of pavement maintenance and reducing the cost of labor and resources. A recently influential branch of this task divides the pavement image into patches and addresses these issues from the perspective of multi-instance learning. However, these methods neglect the correlation between patches and suffer from a low efficiency in the model optimization and inference. Meanwhile, Swin Transformer is able to address both of these issues with its unique strengths. Built upon Swin Transformer, we present a vision Transformer named \textbf{P}avement \textbf{I}mage \textbf{C}lassification \textbf{T}ransformer (\textbf{PicT}) for pavement distress classification. In order to better exploit the discriminative information of pavement images at the patch level, the \textit{Patch Labeling Teacher} is proposed to leverage a teacher model to dynamically generate pseudo labels of patches from image labels during each iteration, and guides the model to learn the discriminative features of patches. The broad classification head of Swin Transformer may dilute the discriminative features of distressed patches in the feature aggregation step due to the small distressed area ratio of the pavement image. To overcome this drawback, we present a \textit{Patch Refiner} to cluster patches into different groups and only select the highest distress-risk group to yield a slim head for the final image classification. We evaluate our method on CQU-BPDD. Extensive results show that \textbf{PicT} outperforms the second-best performed model by a large margin of $+2.4\%$ in P@R on detection task, $+3.9\%$ in $F1$ on recognition task, and 1.8x throughput, while enjoying 7x faster training speed using the same computing resources. Our codes and models have been released on \href{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}.

Extensive results show that \textbf{PicT} outperforms the second-best performed model by a large margin of $+2.4\%$ in P@R on detection task, $+3.9\%$ in $F1$ on recognition task, and 1.8x throughput, while enjoying 7x faster training speed using the same computing resources. Our codes and models have been released on \href{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}.
abstract_zh: 路面病害自动分类有利于提高路面养护效率，降低人力和资源成本。该任务最近有影响力的一个分支将路面图像划分为补丁，并从多实例学习的角度解决这些问题。然而，这些方法忽略了补丁之间的相关性，模型优化和推理的效率较低。同时，Swin Transformer 凭借其独特的优势能够解决这两个问题。基于 Swin Transformer，我们提出了一个名为 \textbf{P}avement \textbf{I}mage \textbf{C}lassification \textbf{T}ransformer (\textbf{PicT}) 的视觉 Transformer，用于路面病害分类。为了更好地利用斑块级别路面图像的判别信息，提出了\textit{Patch Labeling Teacher}，利用教师模型在每次迭代期间从图像标签动态生成斑块的伪标签，并指导模型学习斑块的判别特征。由于路面图像的不良面积比例较小，Swin Transformer 的广泛分类头可能会在特征聚合步骤中淡化不良斑块的判别特征。为了克服这个缺点，我们提出了一个 \textit{Patch Refiner} 将补丁聚类到不同的组中，并且只选择最高的遇险风险组来为最终的图像分类产生一个细长的头。我们在 CQU-BPDD 上评估我们的方法。大量结果表明，\textbf{PicT} 在检测任务上的 P@R 中比第二最佳模型的性能高出 $+2.4\%$，在识别任务上的 $F1$ 中比第二好的模型高出 $+3.9\%$，吞吐量为 1.8 倍，同时使用相同的计算资源，训练速度提高了 7 倍。我们的代码和模型已发布在 \href{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT} 上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PicT，一种轻量级弱监督视觉Transformer模型，用于路面病害分类任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法忽视了路面图像块间的相关性，且模型优化和推理效率低下；Swin Transformer虽具优势，但其分类头可能削弱病害块的判别特征。
AI.method: 设计Patch Labeling Teacher动态生成伪标签以指导特征学习，并引入Patch Refiner通过聚类筛选高风险块组，构建轻量分类头。
AI.result: 在CQU-BPDD数据集上，PicT相较次优模型检测任务P@R提升2.4%，识别任务F1提升3.9%，吞吐量达1.8倍且训练速度加快7倍。
AI.conclusion: PicT通过结构优化和弱监督学习有效提升了路面病害分类的准确率与效率，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: PicT: A Slim Weakly Supervised Vision Transformer for Pavement Distress Classification PicT：用于路面病害分类的细长弱监督视觉转换器 Automatic pavement distress classification facilitates improving the efficiency of pavement maintenance and reducing the cost of labor and resources. A recently influential branch of this task divides the pavement image into patches and addresses these issues from the perspective of multi-instance learning. However, these methods neglect the correlation between patches and suffer from a low efficiency in the model optimization and inference. Meanwhile, Swin Transformer is able to address both of these issues with its unique strengths. Built upon Swin Transformer, we present a vision Transformer named \textbf{P}avement \textbf{I}mage \textbf{C}lassification \textbf{T}ransformer (\textbf{PicT}) for pavement distress classification. In order to better exploit the discriminative information of pavement images at the patch level, the \textit{Patch Labeling Teacher} is proposed to leverage a teacher model to dynamically generate pseudo labels of patches from image labels during each iteration, and guides the model to learn the discriminative features of patches. The broad classification head of Swin Transformer may dilute the discriminative features of distressed patches in the feature aggregation step due to the small distressed area ratio of the pavement image. To overcome this drawback, we present a \textit{Patch Refiner} to cluster patches into different groups and only select the highest distress-risk group to yield a slim head for the final image classification. We evaluate our method on CQU-BPDD. Extensive results show that \textbf{PicT} outperforms the second-best performed model by a large margin of $+2.4\%$ in P@R on detection task, $+3.9\%$ in $F1$ on recognition task, and 1.8x throughput, while enjoying 7x faster training speed using the same computing resources. Our codes and models have been released on \href{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}. 路面病害自动分类有利于提高路面养护效率，降低人力和资源成本。该任务最近有影响力的一个分支将路面图像划分为补丁，并从多实例学习的角度解决这些问题。然而，这些方法忽略了补丁之间的相关性，模型优化和推理的效率较低。同时，Swin Transformer 凭借其独特的优势能够解决这两个问题。基于 Swin Transformer，我们提出了一个名为 \textbf{P}avement \textbf{I}mage \textbf{C}lassification \textbf{T}ransformer (\textbf{PicT}) 的视觉 Transformer，用于路面病害分类。为了更好地利用斑块级别路面图像的判别信息，提出了\textit{Patch Labeling Teacher}，利用教师模型在每次迭代期间从图像标签动态生成斑块的伪标签，并指导模型学习斑块的判别特征。由于路面图像的不良面积比例较小，Swin Transformer 的广泛分类头可能会在特征聚合步骤中淡化不良斑块的判别特征。为了克服这个缺点，我们提出了一个 \textit{Patch Refiner} 将补丁聚类到不同的组中，并且只选择最高的遇险风险组来为最终的图像分类产生一个细长的头。我们在 CQU-BPDD 上评估我们的方法。大量结果表明，\textbf{PicT} 在检测任务上的 P@R 中比第二最佳模型的性能高出 $+2.4\%$，在识别任务上的 $F1$ 中比第二好的模型高出 $+3.9\%$，吞吐量为 1.8 倍，同时使用相同的计算资源，训练速度提高了 7 倍。我们的代码和模型已发布在 \href{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT}{https://github.com/DearCaat/PicT} 上。 本文提出PicT，一种轻量级弱监督视觉Transformer模型，用于路面病害分类任务。 Wenhao Tang,Sheng Huang,Xiaoxian Zhang,Luwen Huangfu cs.CV

authors_text: Anqi Zhu,Qiuhong Ke,Mingming Gong,James Bailey
title: Adaptive Local-Component-aware Graph Convolutional Network for One-shot Skeleton-based Action Recognition
title_zh: 用于一次性基于骨架的动作识别的自适应局部组件感知图卷积网络
abstract: Skeleton-based action recognition receives increasing attention because the skeleton representations reduce the amount of training data by eliminating visual information irrelevant to actions. To further improve the sample efficiency, meta-learning-based one-shot learning solutions were developed for skeleton-based action recognition. These methods find the nearest neighbor according to the similarity between instance-level global average embedding. However, such measurement holds unstable representativity due to inadequate generalized learning on local invariant and noisy features, while intuitively, more fine-grained recognition usually relies on determining key local body movements. To address this limitation, we present the Adaptive Local-Component-aware Graph Convolutional Network, which replaces the comparison metric with a focused sum of similarity measurements on aligned local embedding of action-critical spatial/temporal segments. Comprehensive one-shot experiments on the public benchmark of NTU-RGB+D 120 indicate that our method provides a stronger representation than the global embedding and helps our model reach state-of-the-art.
summary: Skeleton-based action recognition receives increasing attention because the skeleton representations reduce the amount of training data by eliminating visual information irrelevant to actions. To further improve the sample efficiency, meta-learning-based one-shot learning solutions were developed for skeleton-based action recognition. These methods find the nearest neighbor according to the similarity between instance-level global average embedding. However, such measurement holds unstable representativity due to inadequate generalized learning on local invariant and noisy features, while intuitively, more fine-grained recognition usually relies on determining key local body movements. To address this limitation, we present the Adaptive Local-Component-aware Graph Convolutional Network, which replaces the comparison metric with a focused sum of similarity measurements on aligned local embedding of action-critical spatial/temporal segments. Comprehensive one-shot experiments on the public benchmark of NTU-RGB+D 120 indicate that our method provides a stronger representation than the global embedding and helps our model reach state-of-the-art.
abstract_zh: 基于骨架的动作识别受到越来越多的关注，因为骨架表示通过消除与动作无关的视觉信息来减少训练数据量。为了进一步提高样本效率，开发了基于元学习的一次性学习解决方案，用于基于骨架的动作识别。这些方法根据实例级全局平均嵌入之间的相似性找到最近邻居。然而，由于对局部不变性和噪声特征的广义学习不足，这种测量的代表性不稳定，而直观上，更细粒度的识别通常依赖于确定关键的局部身体运动。为了解决这个限制，我们提出了自适应局部组件感知图卷积网络，它用对动作关键空间/时间段的对齐局部嵌入的相似性测量的集中总和来取代比较度量。对 NTU-RGB+D 120 公共基准的全面一次性实验表明，我们的方法提供了比全局嵌入更强的表示，并帮助我们的模型达到了最先进的水平。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自适应局部组件感知图卷积网络，用于解决单次骨骼动作识别问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于全局嵌入的单次学习方法因忽略局部不变特征和噪声导致表示不稳定，而精细动作识别依赖关键局部运动信息。
AI.method: 通过替换全局相似度度量为对齐的局部关键时空片段嵌入的聚焦相似度求和，构建自适应局部组件感知图卷积网络。
AI.result: 在NTU-RGB+D 120数据集上的实验表明，该方法在单次学习任务中优于全局嵌入方法，达到当时最优性能。
AI.conclusion: 局部关键组件的聚焦表示能有效提升骨骼动作识别的样本效率和模型鲁棒性。

authors_text: Haozhi Wang,Qing Wang,Yunfeng Shao,Dong Li,Jianye Hao,Yinchuan Li
title: On the Convergence Theory of Meta Reinforcement Learning with Personalized Policies
title_zh: 元强化学习与个性化策略的收敛理论
abstract: Modern meta-reinforcement learning (Meta-RL) methods are mainly developed based on model-agnostic meta-learning, which performs policy gradient steps across tasks to maximize policy performance. However, the gradient conflict problem is still poorly understood in Meta-RL, which may lead to performance degradation when encountering distinct tasks. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a novel personalized Meta-RL (pMeta-RL) algorithm, which aggregates task-specific personalized policies to update a meta-policy used for all tasks, while maintaining personalized policies to maximize the average return of each task under the constraint of the meta-policy. We also provide the theoretical analysis under the tabular setting, which demonstrates the convergence of our pMeta-RL algorithm. Moreover, we extend the proposed pMeta-RL algorithm to a deep network version based on soft actor-critic, making it suitable for continuous control tasks. Experiment results show that the proposed algorithms outperform other previous Meta-RL algorithms on Gym and MuJoCo suites.
summary: Modern meta-reinforcement learning (Meta-RL) methods are mainly developed based on model-agnostic meta-learning, which performs policy gradient steps across tasks to maximize policy performance. However, the gradient conflict problem is still poorly understood in Meta-RL, which may lead to performance degradation when encountering distinct tasks. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a novel personalized Meta-RL (pMeta-RL) algorithm, which aggregates task-specific personalized policies to update a meta-policy used for all tasks, while maintaining personalized policies to maximize the average return of each task under the constraint of the meta-policy. We also provide the theoretical analysis under the tabular setting, which demonstrates the convergence of our pMeta-RL algorithm. Moreover, we extend the proposed pMeta-RL algorithm to a deep network version based on soft actor-critic, making it suitable for continuous control tasks. Experiment results show that the proposed algorithms outperform other previous Meta-RL algorithms on Gym and MuJoCo suites.
abstract_zh: 现代元强化学习（Meta-RL）方法主要基于与模型无关的元学习开发，它跨任务执行策略梯度步骤以最大化策略性能。然而，Meta-RL 中的梯度冲突问题仍然知之甚少，这可能会导致遇到不同任务时性能下降。为了应对这一挑战，本文提出了一种新颖的个性化元强化学习（pMeta-RL）算法，该算法聚合特定于任务的个性化策略来更新用于所有任务的元策略，同时维护个性化策略以在元策略的约束下最大化每个任务的平均回报。我们还提供了表格设置下的理论分析，这证明了我们的 pMeta-RL 算法的收敛性。此外，我们将所提出的 pMeta-RL 算法扩展到基于软 Actor-Critic 的深度网络版本，使其适用于连续控制任务。实验结果表明，所提出的算法在 Gym 和 MuJoCo 套件上优于之前的其他 Meta-RL 算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为pMeta-RL的个性化元强化学习算法，通过聚合任务特定策略来更新元策略，以解决元强化学习中的梯度冲突问题。
AI.motivation: 现有元强化学习方法在面对不同任务时易出现梯度冲突，导致性能下降，需通过个性化策略平衡元策略与任务特定需求。
AI.method: 设计pMeta-RL算法，在表格环境下理论分析其收敛性，并基于软演员评论家框架扩展至深度网络，适用于连续控制任务。
AI.result: 实验表明，pMeta-RL在Gym和MuJoCo基准测试中优于其他元强化学习算法。
AI.conclusion: pMeta-RL通过个性化策略有效缓解梯度冲突，理论分析与实验验证了其在离散和连续任务中的有效性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为pMeta-RL的个性化元强化学习算法，通过聚合任务特定策略来更新元策略，以解决元强化学习中的梯度冲突问题。

search_text: On the Convergence Theory of Meta Reinforcement Learning with Personalized Policies 元强化学习与个性化策略的收敛理论 Modern meta-reinforcement learning (Meta-RL) methods are mainly developed based on model-agnostic meta-learning, which performs policy gradient steps across tasks to maximize policy performance. However, the gradient conflict problem is still poorly understood in Meta-RL, which may lead to performance degradation when encountering distinct tasks. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a novel personalized Meta-RL (pMeta-RL) algorithm, which aggregates task-specific personalized policies to update a meta-policy used for all tasks, while maintaining personalized policies to maximize the average return of each task under the constraint of the meta-policy. We also provide the theoretical analysis under the tabular setting, which demonstrates the convergence of our pMeta-RL algorithm. Moreover, we extend the proposed pMeta-RL algorithm to a deep network version based on soft actor-critic, making it suitable for continuous control tasks. Experiment results show that the proposed algorithms outperform other previous Meta-RL algorithms on Gym and MuJoCo suites. 现代元强化学习（Meta-RL）方法主要基于与模型无关的元学习开发，它跨任务执行策略梯度步骤以最大化策略性能。然而，Meta-RL 中的梯度冲突问题仍然知之甚少，这可能会导致遇到不同任务时性能下降。为了应对这一挑战，本文提出了一种新颖的个性化元强化学习（pMeta-RL）算法，该算法聚合特定于任务的个性化策略来更新用于所有任务的元策略，同时维护个性化策略以在元策略的约束下最大化每个任务的平均回报。我们还提供了表格设置下的理论分析，这证明了我们的 pMeta-RL 算法的收敛性。此外，我们将所提出的 pMeta-RL 算法扩展到基于软 Actor-Critic 的深度网络版本，使其适用于连续控制任务。实验结果表明，所提出的算法在 Gym 和 MuJoCo 套件上优于之前的其他 Meta-RL 算法。 本文提出了一种名为pMeta-RL的个性化元强化学习算法，通过聚合任务特定策略来更新元策略，以解决元强化学习中的梯度冲突问题。 Haozhi Wang,Qing Wang,Yunfeng Shao,Dong Li,Jianye Hao,Yinchuan Li cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Kangdi Shi,Muhammad Alrabeiah,Jun Chen
title: Progressive with Purpose: Guiding Progressive Inpainting DNNs through Context and Structure
title_zh: 有目的的渐进：通过上下文和结构指导渐进式修复 DNN
abstract: The advent of deep learning in the past decade has significantly helped advance image inpainting. Although achieving promising performance, deep learning-based inpainting algorithms still struggle from the distortion caused by the fusion of structural and contextual features, which are commonly obtained from, respectively, deep and shallow layers of a convolutional encoder. Motivated by this observation, we propose a novel progressive inpainting network that maintains the structural and contextual integrity of a processed image. More specifically, inspired by the Gaussian and Laplacian pyramids, the core of the proposed network is a feature extraction module named GLE. Stacking GLE modules enables the network to extract image features from different image frequency components. This ability is important to maintain structural and contextual integrity, for high frequency components correspond to structural information while low frequency components correspond to contextual information. The proposed network utilizes the GLE features to progressively fill in missing regions in a corrupted image in an iterative manner. Our benchmarking experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves clear improvement in performance over many state-of-the-art inpainting algorithms.
summary: The advent of deep learning in the past decade has significantly helped advance image inpainting. Although achieving promising performance, deep learning-based inpainting algorithms still struggle from the distortion caused by the fusion of structural and contextual features, which are commonly obtained from, respectively, deep and shallow layers of a convolutional encoder. Motivated by this observation, we propose a novel progressive inpainting network that maintains the structural and contextual integrity of a processed image. More specifically, inspired by the Gaussian and Laplacian pyramids, the core of the proposed network is a feature extraction module named GLE. Stacking GLE modules enables the network to extract image features from different image frequency components. This ability is important to maintain structural and contextual integrity, for high frequency components correspond to structural information while low frequency components correspond to contextual information. The proposed network utilizes the GLE features to progressively fill in missing regions in a corrupted image in an iterative manner. Our benchmarking experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves clear improvement in performance over many state-of-the-art inpainting algorithms.
abstract_zh: 过去十年深度学习的出现极大地帮助了图像修复的发展。尽管取得了可喜的性能，但基于深度学习的修复算法仍然面临结构特征和上下文特征融合引起的失真的问题，这些特征通常分别从卷积编码器的深层和浅层获得。受这一观察的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的渐进式修复网络，可以保持处理后图像的结构和上下文完整性。更具体地说，受到高斯金字塔和拉普拉斯金字塔的启发，所提出的网络的核心是一个名为 GLE 的特征提取模块。堆叠 GLE 模块使网络能够从不同图像频率分量中提取图像特征。这种能力对于保持结构和上下文的完整性非常重要，因为高频分量对应于结构信息，而低频分量对应于上下文信息。所提出的网络利用 GLE 特征以迭代方式逐步填充损坏图像中的缺失区域。我们的基准测试实验表明，与许多最先进的修复算法相比，所提出的方法在性能上取得了明显的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种渐进式图像修复网络，通过分离结构和上下文特征来提升修复效果。
AI.motivation: 现有基于深度学习的图像修复算法在融合结构和上下文特征时易产生失真，因结构信息（高频）与上下文信息（低频）通常来自编码器的不同层次。
AI.method: 设计了基于高斯-拉普拉斯金字塔的GLE特征提取模块，通过堆叠GLE模块分离图像不同频率成分，逐步迭代修复缺失区域。
AI.result: 在基准测试中，该方法在多个指标上优于现有先进算法，修复结果更完整自然。
AI.conclusion: 通过频率分离与渐进修复策略，有效保持了图像结构与上下文完整性，为图像修复提供了新思路。

authors_text: Wenhao Yu,Dan Iter,Shuohang Wang,Yichong Xu,Mingxuan Ju,Soumya Sanyal,Chenguang Zhu,Michael Zeng,Meng Jiang
title: Generate rather than Retrieve: Large Language Models are Strong Context Generators
title_zh: 生成而不是检索：大型语言模型是强大的上下文生成器
comment: Accepted at ICLR 2023 (v3, add code and implementation details)
comments: Accepted at ICLR 2023 (v3, add code and implementation details)
abstract: Knowledge-intensive tasks, such as open-domain question answering (QA), require access to a large amount of world or domain knowledge. A common approach for knowledge-intensive tasks is to employ a retrieve-then-read pipeline that first retrieves a handful of relevant contextual documents from an external corpus such as Wikipedia and then predicts an answer conditioned on the retrieved documents. In this paper, we present a novel perspective for solving knowledge-intensive tasks by replacing document retrievers with large language model generators. We call our method generate-then-read (GenRead), which first prompts a large language model to generate contextutal documents based on a given question, and then reads the generated documents to produce the final answer. Furthermore, we propose a novel clustering-based prompting method that selects distinct prompts, resulting in the generated documents that cover different perspectives, leading to better recall over acceptable answers. We conduct extensive experiments on three different knowledge-intensive tasks, including open-domain QA, fact checking, and dialogue system. Notably, GenRead achieves 71.6 and 54.4 exact match scores on TriviaQA and WebQ, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-art retrieve-then-read pipeline DPR-FiD by +4.0 and +3.9, without retrieving any documents from any external knowledge source. Lastly, we demonstrate the model performance can be further improved by combining retrieval and generation. Our code and generated documents can be found at https://github.com/wyu97/GenRead.
summary: Knowledge-intensive tasks, such as open-domain question answering (QA), require access to a large amount of world or domain knowledge. A common approach for knowledge-intensive tasks is to employ a retrieve-then-read pipeline that first retrieves a handful of relevant contextual documents from an external corpus such as Wikipedia and then predicts an answer conditioned on the retrieved documents. In this paper, we present a novel perspective for solving knowledge-intensive tasks by replacing document retrievers with large language model generators. We call our method generate-then-read (GenRead), which first prompts a large language model to generate contextutal documents based on a given question, and then reads the generated documents to produce the final answer. Furthermore, we propose a novel clustering-based prompting method that selects distinct prompts, resulting in the generated documents that cover different perspectives, leading to better recall over acceptable answers. We conduct extensive experiments on three different knowledge-intensive tasks, including open-domain QA, fact checking, and dialogue system. Notably, GenRead achieves 71.6 and 54.4 exact match scores on TriviaQA and WebQ, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-art retrieve-then-read pipeline DPR-FiD by +4.0 and +3.9, without retrieving any documents from any external knowledge source. Lastly, we demonstrate the model performance can be further improved by combining retrieval and generation. Our code and generated documents can be found at https://github.com/wyu97/GenRead.
abstract_zh: 知识密集型任务，例如开放域问答（QA），需要访问大量的世界或领域知识。知识密集型任务的常见方法是采用检索然后读取的管道，该管道首先从维基百科等外部语料库中检索一些相关的上下文文档，然后根据检索到的文档预测答案。在本文中，我们提出了一种通过用大型语言模型生成器替换文档检索器来解决知识密集型任务的新颖视角。我们将我们的方法称为“生成然后读取”（GenRead），它首先提示大型语言模型根据给定问题生成上下文文档，然后读取生成的文档以产生最终答案。此外，我们提出了一种新颖的基于聚类的提示方法，该方法选择不同的提示，从而生成涵盖不同观点的文档，从而更好地回忆可接受的答案。我们对三种不同的知识密集型任务进行了广泛的实验，包括开放域问答、事实检查和对话系统。值得注意的是，GenRead 在 TriviaQA 和 WebQ 上获得了 71.6 和 54.4 的精确匹配分数，显着优于最先进的检索然后读取管道 DPR-FiD +4.0 和 +3.9，而无需从任何外部知识源检索任何文档。最后，我们证明通过结合检索和生成可以进一步提高模型性能。我们的代码和生成的文档可以在 https://github.com/wyu97/GenRead 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新型知识密集型任务解决方法GenRead，通过大语言模型生成上下文文档替代传统检索方式。
AI.motivation: 传统检索-阅读方法受限于外部知识库质量和检索效果，而大语言模型具备强大的上下文生成能力。
AI.method: 采用生成-阅读（GenRead）框架，先用大模型生成多视角上下文文档，再结合聚类提示方法提升答案召回率。
AI.result: 在TriviaQA和WebQ任务中分别达到71.6和54.4的精确匹配分数，较DPR-FiD提升4.0和3.9分。
AI.conclusion: 生成方法可有效替代检索，且与检索方法结合能进一步提升模型性能。

Notably, GenRead achieves 71.6 and 54.4 exact match scores on TriviaQA and WebQ, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-art retrieve-then-read pipeline DPR-FiD by +4.0 and +3.9, without retrieving any documents from any external knowledge source. Lastly, we demonstrate the model performance can be further improved by combining retrieval and generation. Our code and generated documents can be found at https://github.com/wyu97/GenRead. 知识密集型任务，例如开放域问答（QA），需要访问大量的世界或领域知识。知识密集型任务的常见方法是采用检索然后读取的管道，该管道首先从维基百科等外部语料库中检索一些相关的上下文文档，然后根据检索到的文档预测答案。在本文中，我们提出了一种通过用大型语言模型生成器替换文档检索器来解决知识密集型任务的新颖视角。我们将我们的方法称为“生成然后读取”（GenRead），它首先提示大型语言模型根据给定问题生成上下文文档，然后读取生成的文档以产生最终答案。此外，我们提出了一种新颖的基于聚类的提示方法，该方法选择不同的提示，从而生成涵盖不同观点的文档，从而更好地回忆可接受的答案。我们对三种不同的知识密集型任务进行了广泛的实验，包括开放域问答、事实检查和对话系统。值得注意的是，GenRead 在 TriviaQA 和 WebQ 上获得了 71.6 和 54.4 的精确匹配分数，显着优于最先进的检索然后读取管道 DPR-FiD +4.0 和 +3.9，而无需从任何外部知识源检索任何文档。最后，我们证明通过结合检索和生成可以进一步提高模型性能。我们的代码和生成的文档可以在 https://github.com/wyu97/GenRead 找到。 本文提出一种新型知识密集型任务解决方法GenRead，通过大语言模型生成上下文文档替代传统检索方式。 Wenhao Yu,Dan Iter,Shuohang Wang,Yichong Xu,Mingxuan Ju,Soumya Sanyal,Chenguang Zhu,Michael Zeng,Meng Jiang cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Jirong Yi,Qiaosheng Zhang,Zhen Chen,Qiao Liu,Wei Shao
title: Mutual Information Learned Classifiers: an Information-theoretic Viewpoint of Training Deep Learning Classification Systems
title_zh: 互信息学习分类器：训练深度学习分类系统的信息论观点
comment: 22 pages, 17 figures, 3 tables, 5 theorems
comments: 22 pages, 17 figures, 3 tables, 5 theorems
abstract: Deep learning systems have been reported to achieve state-of-the-art performances in many applications, and a key is the existence of well trained classifiers on benchmark datasets. As a main-stream loss function, the cross entropy can easily lead us to find models which demonstrate severe overfitting behavior. In this paper, we show that the existing cross entropy loss minimization problem essentially learns the label conditional entropy (CE) of the underlying data distribution of the dataset. However, the CE learned in this way does not characterize well the information shared by the label and the input. In this paper, we propose a mutual information learning framework where we train deep neural network classifiers via learning the mutual information between the label and the input. Theoretically, we give the population classification error lower bound in terms of the mutual information. In addition, we derive the mutual information lower and upper bounds for a concrete binary classification data model in $\mathbb{R}^n$, and also the error probability lower bound in this scenario. Empirically, we conduct extensive experiments on several benchmark datasets to support our theory. The mutual information learned classifiers (MILCs) achieve far better generalization performances than the conditional entropy learned classifiers (CELCs) with an improvement which can exceed more than 10\% in testing accuracy.
summary: Deep learning systems have been reported to achieve state-of-the-art performances in many applications, and a key is the existence of well trained classifiers on benchmark datasets. As a main-stream loss function, the cross entropy can easily lead us to find models which demonstrate severe overfitting behavior. In this paper, we show that the existing cross entropy loss minimization problem essentially learns the label conditional entropy (CE) of the underlying data distribution of the dataset. However, the CE learned in this way does not characterize well the information shared by the label and the input. In this paper, we propose a mutual information learning framework where we train deep neural network classifiers via learning the mutual information between the label and the input. Theoretically, we give the population classification error lower bound in terms of the mutual information. In addition, we derive the mutual information lower and upper bounds for a concrete binary classification data model in $\mathbb{R}^n$, and also the error probability lower bound in this scenario. Empirically, we conduct extensive experiments on several benchmark datasets to support our theory. The mutual information learned classifiers (MILCs) achieve far better generalization performances than the conditional entropy learned classifiers (CELCs) with an improvement which can exceed more than 10\% in testing accuracy.
abstract_zh: 据报道，深度学习系统在许多应用中实现了最先进的性能，其中一个关键是在基准数据集上存在训练有素的分类器。作为主流损失函数，交叉熵可以很容易地引导我们找到表现出严重过拟合行为的模型。在本文中，我们表明现有的交叉熵损失最小化问题本质上是学习数据集底层数据分布的标签条件熵（CE）。然而，以这种方式学习的CE并不能很好地表征标签和输入共享的信息。在本文中，我们提出了一种互信息学习框架，通过学习标签和输入之间的互信息来训练深度神经网络分类器。理论上，我们根据互信息给出总体分类误差下界。此外，我们还导出了 $\mathbb{R}^n$ 中具体二元分类数据模型的互信息下界和上限，以及该场景中的错误概率下界。根据经验，我们对几个基准数据集进行了广泛的实验来支持我们的理论。互信息学习分类器 (MILC) 比条件熵学习分类器 (CELC) 实现了更好的泛化性能，测试精度提高超过 10%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于互信息学习的深度分类框架，从信息论角度改进传统交叉熵损失函数的分类器训练方法。
AI.motivation: 传统交叉熵损失函数易导致过拟合，且未能有效表征标签与输入间的共享信息，需寻找更优的信息理论视角的分类方法。
AI.method: 构建互信息学习框架，通过最大化标签与输入间的互信息训练深度网络，并推导理论下界及二分类场景的互信息上下界，结合实验验证。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上，互信息学习分类器（MILCs）相较条件熵学习分类器（CELCs）测试准确率提升超10%，泛化性能显著更优。
AI.conclusion: 互信息学习能有效捕捉标签与输入的共享信息，为深度分类系统提供理论保证与实践改进，优于传统交叉熵方法。

authors_text: Hui Bai,Ruimin Shen,Yue Lin,Botian Xu,Ran Cheng
title: Lamarckian Platform: Pushing the Boundaries of Evolutionary Reinforcement Learning towards Asynchronous Commercial Games
title_zh: 拉马克平台：将进化强化学习的界限推向异步商业游戏
abstract: Despite the emerging progress of integrating evolutionary computation into reinforcement learning, the absence of a high-performance platform endowing composability and massive parallelism causes non-trivial difficulties for research and applications related to asynchronous commercial games. Here we introduce Lamarckian - an open-source platform featuring support for evolutionary reinforcement learning scalable to distributed computing resources. To improve the training speed and data efficiency, Lamarckian adopts optimized communication methods and an asynchronous evolutionary reinforcement learning workflow. To meet the demand for an asynchronous interface by commercial games and various methods, Lamarckian tailors an asynchronous Markov Decision Process interface and designs an object-oriented software architecture with decoupled modules. In comparison with the state-of-the-art RLlib, we empirically demonstrate the unique advantages of Lamarckian on benchmark tests with up to 6000 CPU cores: i) both the sampling efficiency and training speed are doubled when running PPO on Google football game; ii) the training speed is 13 times faster when running PBT+PPO on Pong game. Moreover, we also present two use cases: i) how Lamarckian is applied to generating behavior-diverse game AI; ii) how Lamarckian is applied to game balancing tests for an asynchronous commercial game.
summary: Despite the emerging progress of integrating evolutionary computation into reinforcement learning, the absence of a high-performance platform endowing composability and massive parallelism causes non-trivial difficulties for research and applications related to asynchronous commercial games. Here we introduce Lamarckian - an open-source platform featuring support for evolutionary reinforcement learning scalable to distributed computing resources. To improve the training speed and data efficiency, Lamarckian adopts optimized communication methods and an asynchronous evolutionary reinforcement learning workflow. To meet the demand for an asynchronous interface by commercial games and various methods, Lamarckian tailors an asynchronous Markov Decision Process interface and designs an object-oriented software architecture with decoupled modules. In comparison with the state-of-the-art RLlib, we empirically demonstrate the unique advantages of Lamarckian on benchmark tests with up to 6000 CPU cores: i) both the sampling efficiency and training speed are doubled when running PPO on Google football game; ii) the training speed is 13 times faster when running PBT+PPO on Pong game. Moreover, we also present two use cases: i) how Lamarckian is applied to generating behavior-diverse game AI; ii) how Lamarckian is applied to game balancing tests for an asynchronous commercial game.
abstract_zh: 尽管将进化计算集成到强化学习中取得了新的进展，但缺乏具有可组合性和大规模并行性的高性能平台给异步商业游戏相关的研究和应用带来了不小的困难。在这里，我们介绍 Lamarckian - 一个开源平台，支持可扩展至分布式计算资源的进化强化学习。为了提高训练速度和数据效率，Lamarckian 采用优化的通信方法和异步进化强化学习工作流程。为了满足商业游戏和各种方法对异步接口的需求，Lamarckian定制了异步马尔可夫决策过程接口，并设计了面向对象的模块解耦的软件架构。与最先进的 RLlib 相比，我们在多达 6000 个 CPU 核心的基准测试中实证证明了 Lamarckian 的独特优势：i）在 Google 足球比赛上运行 PPO 时，采样效率和训练速度均提高了一倍； ii) 在 Pong 游戏上运行 PBT+PPO 时，训练速度快了 13 倍。此外，我们还提出了两个用例：i）如何应用拉马克矩阵来生成行为多样化的游戏人工智能； ii) 如何将 Lamarckian 应用于异步商业游戏的游戏平衡测试。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Lamarckian平台，旨在解决异步商业游戏中进化强化学习的高性能计算需求，通过优化通信和异步流程提升训练效率。
AI.motivation: 现有平台缺乏支持异步商业游戏的高性能、可扩展架构，导致进化强化学习在分布式计算中面临效率瓶颈。
AI.method: 设计异步MDP接口与模块化架构，采用优化通信方法及异步进化强化学习流程，适配分布式计算资源。
AI.result: 在Google足球游戏和Pong游戏中，Lamarckian相比RLlib实现采样效率和训练速度翻倍及13倍加速，并展示AI生成与游戏平衡测试案例。
AI.conclusion: Lamarckian平台通过异步架构和优化方法显著提升进化强化学习效率，为异步商业游戏提供可行解决方案。

Moreover, we also present two use cases: i) how Lamarckian is applied to generating behavior-diverse game AI; ii) how Lamarckian is applied to game balancing tests for an asynchronous commercial game. 尽管将进化计算集成到强化学习中取得了新的进展，但缺乏具有可组合性和大规模并行性的高性能平台给异步商业游戏相关的研究和应用带来了不小的困难。在这里，我们介绍 Lamarckian - 一个开源平台，支持可扩展至分布式计算资源的进化强化学习。为了提高训练速度和数据效率，Lamarckian 采用优化的通信方法和异步进化强化学习工作流程。为了满足商业游戏和各种方法对异步接口的需求，Lamarckian定制了异步马尔可夫决策过程接口，并设计了面向对象的模块解耦的软件架构。与最先进的 RLlib 相比，我们在多达 6000 个 CPU 核心的基准测试中实证证明了 Lamarckian 的独特优势：i）在 Google 足球比赛上运行 PPO 时，采样效率和训练速度均提高了一倍； ii) 在 Pong 游戏上运行 PBT+PPO 时，训练速度快了 13 倍。此外，我们还提出了两个用例：i）如何应用拉马克矩阵来生成行为多样化的游戏人工智能； ii) 如何将 Lamarckian 应用于异步商业游戏的游戏平衡测试。 本文提出Lamarckian平台，旨在解决异步商业游戏中进化强化学习的高性能计算需求，通过优化通信和异步流程提升训练效率。 Hui Bai,Ruimin Shen,Yue Lin,Botian Xu,Ran Cheng cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.NE

authors_text: Sabir Hossain,Xianke Lin
title: Uncertainty-Aware Tightly-Coupled GPS Fused LIO-SLAM
title_zh: 不确定性感知紧耦合 GPS 融合 LIO-SLAM
comment: 10 pages, 11 figures
comments: 10 pages, 11 figures
abstract: Delivery robots aim to achieve high precision to facilitate complete autonomy. A precise three-dimensional point cloud map of sidewalk surroundings is required to estimate self-location. With or without the loop closing method, the cumulative error increases gradually after mapping for larger urban or city maps due to sensor drift. Therefore, there is a high risk of using the drifted or misaligned map. This article presented a technique for fusing GPS to update the 3D point cloud and eliminate cumulative error. The proposed method shows outstanding results in quantitative comparison and qualitative evaluation with other existing methods.
summary: Delivery robots aim to achieve high precision to facilitate complete autonomy. A precise three-dimensional point cloud map of sidewalk surroundings is required to estimate self-location. With or without the loop closing method, the cumulative error increases gradually after mapping for larger urban or city maps due to sensor drift. Therefore, there is a high risk of using the drifted or misaligned map. This article presented a technique for fusing GPS to update the 3D point cloud and eliminate cumulative error. The proposed method shows outstanding results in quantitative comparison and qualitative evaluation with other existing methods.
abstract_zh: 送货机器人的目标是实现高精度，以实现完全自主。需要人行道周围环境的精确三维点云图来估计自我定位。无论是否采用闭环方法，在绘制较大的城市或城市地图后，由于传感器漂移，累积误差会逐渐增加。因此，使用漂移或未对齐的地图的风险很高。本文提出了一种融合 GPS 来更新 3D 点云并消除累积误差的技术。该方法与其他现有方法在定量比较和定性评价方面显示出优异的结果。

AI.tldr: 提出一种融合GPS的紧耦合LIO-SLAM方法，以解决送餐机器人地图构建中的累积误差问题，提高定位精度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在较大城市地图中因传感器漂移导致累积误差，使用此类地图存在高风险，需通过GPS融合来消除误差。
AI.method: 提出一种结合不确定性感知机制的紧耦合GPS融合LIO-SLAM方法，通过实时更新三维点云地图来消除累积误差。
AI.result: 定量比较和定性评估显示，该方法在精度和鲁棒性上优于现有方法，有效减少了累积误差。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了传感器漂移导致的累积误差问题，适用于大范围城市环境下的高精度SLAM任务。

authors_text: Michael S. Yao,Allison Chae,Matthew T. MacLean,Anurag Verma,Jeffrey Duda,James Gee,Drew A. Torigian,Daniel Rader,Charles Kahn,Walter R. Witschey,Hersh Sagreiya
title: SynthA1c: Towards Clinically Interpretable Patient Representations for Diabetes Risk Stratification
title_zh: SynthA1c：针对糖尿病风险分层的临床可解释的患者表征
comment: 12 pages. Accepted to PRIME MICCAI 2023
comments: 12 pages. Accepted to PRIME MICCAI 2023
abstract: Early diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) is crucial to enable timely therapeutic interventions and lifestyle modifications. As the time available for clinical office visits shortens and medical imaging data become more widely available, patient image data could be used to opportunistically identify patients for additional T2DM diagnostic workup by physicians. We investigated whether image-derived phenotypic data could be leveraged in tabular learning classifier models to predict T2DM risk in an automated fashion to flag high-risk patients without the need for additional blood laboratory measurements. In contrast to traditional binary classifiers, we leverage neural networks and decision tree models to represent patient data as 'SynthA1c' latent variables, which mimic blood hemoglobin A1c empirical lab measurements, that achieve sensitivities as high as 87.6%. To evaluate how SynthA1c models may generalize to other patient populations, we introduce a novel generalizable metric that uses vanilla data augmentation techniques to predict model performance on input out-of-domain covariates. We show that image-derived phenotypes and physical examination data together can accurately predict diabetes risk as a means of opportunistic risk stratification enabled by artificial intelligence and medical imaging. Our code is available at https://github.com/allisonjchae/DMT2RiskAssessment.
summary: Early diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) is crucial to enable timely therapeutic interventions and lifestyle modifications. As the time available for clinical office visits shortens and medical imaging data become more widely available, patient image data could be used to opportunistically identify patients for additional T2DM diagnostic workup by physicians. We investigated whether image-derived phenotypic data could be leveraged in tabular learning classifier models to predict T2DM risk in an automated fashion to flag high-risk patients without the need for additional blood laboratory measurements. In contrast to traditional binary classifiers, we leverage neural networks and decision tree models to represent patient data as 'SynthA1c' latent variables, which mimic blood hemoglobin A1c empirical lab measurements, that achieve sensitivities as high as 87.6%. To evaluate how SynthA1c models may generalize to other patient populations, we introduce a novel generalizable metric that uses vanilla data augmentation techniques to predict model performance on input out-of-domain covariates. We show that image-derived phenotypes and physical examination data together can accurately predict diabetes risk as a means of opportunistic risk stratification enabled by artificial intelligence and medical imaging. Our code is available at https://github.com/allisonjchae/DMT2RiskAssessment.
abstract_zh: 2 型糖尿病 (T2DM) 的早期诊断对于及时进行治疗干预和改变生活方式至关重要。随着临床就诊时间的缩短以及医学影像数据的广泛使用，患者图像数据可用于机会性地识别患者，以便医生进行额外的 T2DM 诊断检查。我们研究了是否可以在表格学习分类器模型中利用图像衍生的表型数据以自动方式预测 T2DM 风险，从而标记高危患者，而无需额外的血液实验室测量。与传统的二元分类器相比，我们利用神经网络和决策树模型将患者数据表示为“SynthA1c”潜在变量，它模仿血液血红蛋白 A1c 经验实验室测量结果，灵敏度高达 87.6%。为了评估 SynthA1c 模型如何推广到其他患者群体，我们引入了一种新颖的可推广指标，该指标使用普通数据增强技术来预测输入域外协变量的模型性能。我们证明，图像衍生的表型和体检数据一起可以准确预测糖尿病风险，作为人工智能和医学成像实现机会性风险分层的一种手段。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/allisonjchae/DMT2RiskAssessment 获取。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于医学影像和临床数据的糖尿病风险分层方法SynthA1c，通过机器学习模型预测2型糖尿病风险。
AI.motivation: 早期诊断2型糖尿病对及时干预至关重要，但传统方法依赖血液检测。随着影像数据普及，需探索无需实验室检测的自动化风险预测方案。
AI.method: 结合神经网络与决策树模型，将影像衍生表型与体检数据转化为模拟糖化血红蛋白（A1c）的潜变量SynthA1c，并引入数据增强技术评估模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 模型在高风险患者识别中达到87.6%的敏感度，且通过新指标验证了对不同人群的泛化能力，证明影像数据与体检数据联合预测的有效性。
AI.conclusion: SynthA1c方法通过人工智能和医学影像实现糖尿病机会性风险分层，为临床提供无需血液检测的自动化风险评估工具。

authors_text: Rana Soltani Zarrin,Katsu Yamane,Rianna Jitosho
title: Hybrid Learning- and Model-Based Planning and Control of In-Hand Manipulation
title_zh: 基于混合学习和模型的手动操作规划和控制
comment: 7 pages
comments: 7 pages
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical framework for planning and control of in-hand manipulation of a rigid object involving grasp changes using fully-actuated multifingered robotic hands. While the framework can be applied to the general dexterous manipulation, we focus on a more complex definition of in-hand manipulation, where at the goal pose the hand has to reach a grasp suitable for using the object as a tool. The high level planner determines the object trajectory as well as the grasp changes, i.e. adding, removing, or sliding fingers, to be executed by the low-level controller. While the grasp sequence is planned online by a learning-based policy to adapt to variations, the trajectory planner and the low-level controller for object tracking and contact force control are exclusively model-based to robustly realize the plan. By infusing the knowledge about the physics of the problem and the low-level controller into the grasp planner, it learns to successfully generate grasps similar to those generated by model-based optimization approaches, obviating the high computation cost of online running of such methods to account for variations. By performing experiments in physics simulation for realistic tool use scenarios, we show the success of our method on different tool-use tasks and dexterous hand models. Additionally, we show that this hybrid method offers more robustness to trajectory and task variations compared to a model-based method.
summary: This paper presents a hierarchical framework for planning and control of in-hand manipulation of a rigid object involving grasp changes using fully-actuated multifingered robotic hands. While the framework can be applied to the general dexterous manipulation, we focus on a more complex definition of in-hand manipulation, where at the goal pose the hand has to reach a grasp suitable for using the object as a tool. The high level planner determines the object trajectory as well as the grasp changes, i.e. adding, removing, or sliding fingers, to be executed by the low-level controller. While the grasp sequence is planned online by a learning-based policy to adapt to variations, the trajectory planner and the low-level controller for object tracking and contact force control are exclusively model-based to robustly realize the plan. By infusing the knowledge about the physics of the problem and the low-level controller into the grasp planner, it learns to successfully generate grasps similar to those generated by model-based optimization approaches, obviating the high computation cost of online running of such methods to account for variations. By performing experiments in physics simulation for realistic tool use scenarios, we show the success of our method on different tool-use tasks and dexterous hand models. Additionally, we show that this hybrid method offers more robustness to trajectory and task variations compared to a model-based method.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个分层框架，用于规划和控制刚性物体的手动操作，涉及使用全驱动多指机器人手进行抓握变化。虽然该框架可以应用于一般的灵巧操作，但我们专注于手动操作的更复杂的定义，其中在目标姿势时，手必须达到适合使用物体作为工具的抓握力。高层规划器确定对象轨迹以及抓取变化，即添加、移除或滑动手指，由低层控制器执行。虽然抓取序列是通过基于学习的策略在线规划以适应变化，但用于对象跟踪和接触力控制的轨迹规划器和低级控制器完全基于模型，以稳健地实现该计划。通过将有关问题物理和低级控制器的知识注入抓取规划器中，它学会了成功生成类似于基于模型的优化方法生成的抓取，从而避免了在线运行此类方法来解释变化的高计算成本。通过对真实工具使用场景进行物理模拟实验，我们展示了我们的方法在不同工具使用任务和灵巧手模型上的成功。此外，我们还表明，与基于模型的方法相比，这种混合方法对轨迹和任务变化具有更强的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合学习与模型的混合方法，用于多指机械手在手操作中抓取变化的规划与控制。
AI.motivation: 传统模型方法在处理复杂抓取变化时计算成本高，而纯学习方法缺乏物理约束保证鲁棒性，需结合两者优势。
AI.method: 分层框架：高层用学习策略在线规划抓取变化序列，底层采用模型驱动方法实现轨迹跟踪与力控制，注入物理知识降低计算成本。
AI.result: 在物理仿真中验证了方法在多种工具使用任务中的有效性，相比纯模型方法展现出更强的轨迹与任务变化鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 混合方法通过融合学习策略的适应性与模型控制的鲁棒性，为复杂在手操作提供了高效可靠的解决方案。
