authors_text: Dieqiao Feng,Carla P. Gomes,Bart Selman
title: Graph Value Iteration
title_zh: 图值迭代
abstract: In recent years, deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) has been successful in various combinatorial search domains, such as two-player games and scientific discovery. However, directly applying deep RL in planning domains is still challenging. One major difficulty is that without a human-crafted heuristic function, reward signals remain zero unless the learning framework discovers any solution plan. Search space becomes \emph{exponentially larger} as the minimum length of plans grows, which is a serious limitation for planning instances with a minimum plan length of hundreds to thousands of steps. Previous learning frameworks that augment graph search with deep neural networks and extra generated subgoals have achieved success in various challenging planning domains. However, generating useful subgoals requires extensive domain knowledge. We propose a domain-independent method that augments graph search with graph value iteration to solve hard planning instances that are out of reach for domain-specialized solvers. In particular, instead of receiving learning signals only from discovered plans, our approach also learns from failed search attempts where no goal state has been reached. The graph value iteration component can exploit the graph structure of local search space and provide more informative learning signals. We also show how we use a curriculum strategy to smooth the learning process and perform a full analysis of how graph value iteration scales and enables learning.
summary: In recent years, deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) has been successful in various combinatorial search domains, such as two-player games and scientific discovery. However, directly applying deep RL in planning domains is still challenging. One major difficulty is that without a human-crafted heuristic function, reward signals remain zero unless the learning framework discovers any solution plan. Search space becomes \emph{exponentially larger} as the minimum length of plans grows, which is a serious limitation for planning instances with a minimum plan length of hundreds to thousands of steps. Previous learning frameworks that augment graph search with deep neural networks and extra generated subgoals have achieved success in various challenging planning domains. However, generating useful subgoals requires extensive domain knowledge. We propose a domain-independent method that augments graph search with graph value iteration to solve hard planning instances that are out of reach for domain-specialized solvers. In particular, instead of receiving learning signals only from discovered plans, our approach also learns from failed search attempts where no goal state has been reached. The graph value iteration component can exploit the graph structure of local search space and provide more informative learning signals. We also show how we use a curriculum strategy to smooth the learning process and perform a full analysis of how graph value iteration scales and enables learning.
abstract_zh: 近年来，深度强化学习（RL）在各种组合搜索领域取得了成功，例如两人游戏和科学发现。然而，在规划领域直接应用深度强化学习仍然具有挑战性。一个主要困难是，如果没有人工设计的启发式函数，奖励信号将保持为零，除非学习框架发现任何解决方案。随着计划的最小长度的增长，搜索空间变得\emph{指数级更大}，这对于最小计划长度为数百到数千步的规划实例来说是一个严重的限制。以前的学习框架通过深度神经网络和额外生成的子目标来增强图搜索，已经在各种具有挑战性的规划领域取得了成功。然而，生成有用的子目标需要广泛的领域知识。我们提出了一种与域无关的方法，通过图值迭代来增强图搜索，以解决领域专用求解器无法解决的硬规划实例。特别是，我们的方法不是仅从发现的计划中接收学习信号，而是从尚未达到目标状态的失败搜索尝试中学习。图值迭代组件可以利用局部搜索空间的图结构并提供更多信息的学习信号。我们还展示了如何使用课程策略来平滑学习过程，并对图值迭代如何扩展和实现学习进行全面分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于图值迭代的领域无关方法，通过结合图搜索与价值迭代，解决传统规划领域中因搜索空间过大而难以处理的复杂规划问题。
AI.motivation: 深度强化学习在规划领域应用时面临搜索空间指数级增长的问题，且依赖人工设计的启发式函数或领域知识生成子目标，限制了其在大规模规划任务中的效果。
AI.method: 提出图值迭代算法，利用图结构分析局部搜索空间，从成功和失败的搜索尝试中学习价值函数，并引入课程学习策略优化训练过程。
AI.result: 该方法在复杂规划实例中表现出优于领域专用求解器的性能，且通过实验分析验证了图值迭代在可扩展性和学习效率上的优势。
AI.conclusion: 图值迭代提供了一种无需领域知识的通用规划框架，通过结合图结构信息与失败经验，显著提升了大规模规划问题的求解能力。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于图值迭代的领域无关方法，通过结合图搜索与价值迭代，解决传统规划领域中因搜索空间过大而难以处理的复杂规划问题。
motivation: 深度强化学习在规划领域应用时面临搜索空间指数级增长的问题，且依赖人工设计的启发式函数或领域知识生成子目标，限制了其在大规模规划任务中的效果。
method: 提出图值迭代算法，利用图结构分析局部搜索空间，从成功和失败的搜索尝试中学习价值函数，并引入课程学习策略优化训练过程。
result: 该方法在复杂规划实例中表现出优于领域专用求解器的性能，且通过实验分析验证了图值迭代在可扩展性和学习效率上的优势。
conclusion: 图值迭代提供了一种无需领域知识的通用规划框架，通过结合图结构信息与失败经验，显著提升了大规模规划问题的求解能力。
search_text: Graph Value Iteration 图值迭代 In recent years, deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) has been successful in various combinatorial search domains, such as two-player games and scientific discovery. However, directly applying deep RL in planning domains is still challenging. One major difficulty is that without a human-crafted heuristic function, reward signals remain zero unless the learning framework discovers any solution plan. Search space becomes \emph{exponentially larger} as the minimum length of plans grows, which is a serious limitation for planning instances with a minimum plan length of hundreds to thousands of steps. Previous learning frameworks that augment graph search with deep neural networks and extra generated subgoals have achieved success in various challenging planning domains. However, generating useful subgoals requires extensive domain knowledge. We propose a domain-independent method that augments graph search with graph value iteration to solve hard planning instances that are out of reach for domain-specialized solvers. In particular, instead of receiving learning signals only from discovered plans, our approach also learns from failed search attempts where no goal state has been reached. The graph value iteration component can exploit the graph structure of local search space and provide more informative learning signals. We also show how we use a curriculum strategy to smooth the learning process and perform a full analysis of how graph value iteration scales and enables learning. 近年来，深度强化学习（RL）在各种组合搜索领域取得了成功，例如两人游戏和科学发现。然而，在规划领域直接应用深度强化学习仍然具有挑战性。一个主要困难是，如果没有人工设计的启发式函数，奖励信号将保持为零，除非学习框架发现任何解决方案。随着计划的最小长度的增长，搜索空间变得\emph{指数级更大}，这对于最小计划长度为数百到数千步的规划实例来说是一个严重的限制。以前的学习框架通过深度神经网络和额外生成的子目标来增强图搜索，已经在各种具有挑战性的规划领域取得了成功。然而，生成有用的子目标需要广泛的领域知识。我们提出了一种与域无关的方法，通过图值迭代来增强图搜索，以解决领域专用求解器无法解决的硬规划实例。特别是，我们的方法不是仅从发现的计划中接收学习信号，而是从尚未达到目标状态的失败搜索尝试中学习。图值迭代组件可以利用局部搜索空间的图结构并提供更多信息的学习信号。我们还展示了如何使用课程策略来平滑学习过程，并对图值迭代如何扩展和实现学习进行全面分析。 本文提出一种基于图值迭代的领域无关方法，通过结合图搜索与价值迭代，解决传统规划领域中因搜索空间过大而难以处理的复杂规划问题。 Dieqiao Feng,Carla P. Gomes,Bart Selman cs.AI

authors_text: Haohong Liao,Silin Zheng,Xuelin Shen,Mark Junjie Li,Xu Wang
title: Semi-automatic Data Annotation System for Multi-Target Multi-Camera Vehicle Tracking
title_zh: 多目标多摄像头车辆跟踪半自动数据标注系统
comment: 9 pages, 10 figures
comments: 9 pages, 10 figures
abstract: Multi-target multi-camera tracking (MTMCT) plays an important role in intelligent video analysis, surveillance video retrieval, and other application scenarios. Nowadays, the deep-learning-based MTMCT has been the mainstream and has achieved fascinating improvements regarding tracking accuracy and efficiency. However, according to our investigation, the lacking of datasets focusing on real-world application scenarios limits the further improvements for current learning-based MTMCT models. Specifically, the learning-based MTMCT models training by common datasets usually cannot achieve satisfactory results in real-world application scenarios. Motivated by this, this paper presents a semi-automatic data annotation system to facilitate the real-world MTMCT dataset establishment. The proposed system first employs a deep-learning-based single-camera trajectory generation method to automatically extract trajectories from surveillance videos. Subsequently, the system provides a recommendation list in the following manual cross-camera trajectory matching process. The recommendation list is generated based on side information, including camera location, timestamp relation, and background scene. In the experimental stage, extensive results further demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed system.
summary: Multi-target multi-camera tracking (MTMCT) plays an important role in intelligent video analysis, surveillance video retrieval, and other application scenarios. Nowadays, the deep-learning-based MTMCT has been the mainstream and has achieved fascinating improvements regarding tracking accuracy and efficiency. However, according to our investigation, the lacking of datasets focusing on real-world application scenarios limits the further improvements for current learning-based MTMCT models. Specifically, the learning-based MTMCT models training by common datasets usually cannot achieve satisfactory results in real-world application scenarios. Motivated by this, this paper presents a semi-automatic data annotation system to facilitate the real-world MTMCT dataset establishment. The proposed system first employs a deep-learning-based single-camera trajectory generation method to automatically extract trajectories from surveillance videos. Subsequently, the system provides a recommendation list in the following manual cross-camera trajectory matching process. The recommendation list is generated based on side information, including camera location, timestamp relation, and background scene. In the experimental stage, extensive results further demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed system.
abstract_zh: 多目标多摄像机跟踪（MTMCT）在智能视频分析、监控视频检索等应用场景中发挥着重要作用。如今，基于深度学习的MTMCT已成为主流，并在跟踪精度和效率方面取得了令人瞩目的进步。然而，根据我们的调查，缺乏关注实际应用场景的数据集限制了当前基于学习的 MTMCT 模型的进一步改进。具体来说，通过通用数据集训练的基于学习的 MTMCT 模型通常无法在实际应用场景中取得令人满意的结果。受此启发，本文提出了一种半自动数据注释系统，以促进现实世界 MTMCT 数据集的建立。该系统首先采用基于深度学习的单摄像头轨迹生成方法从监控视频中自动提取轨迹。随后，系统在接下来的手动跨相机轨迹匹配过程中提供推荐列表。推荐列表是根据辅助信息生成的，包括相机位置、时间戳关系和背景场景。在实验阶段，大量结果进一步证明了所提出系统的效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种半自动数据标注系统，用于多目标多摄像头车辆跟踪的现实场景数据集构建。
AI.motivation: 现有的基于深度学习的多目标多摄像头跟踪模型在现实场景中的表现不佳，主要原因是缺乏针对现实应用场景的数据集。
AI.method: 该系统首先利用基于深度学习的单摄像头轨迹生成方法从监控视频中自动提取轨迹，然后在手动跨摄像头轨迹匹配过程中提供基于摄像头位置、时间戳关系和背景场景的推荐列表。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该系统在建立现实场景数据集方面具有较高的效率。
AI.conclusion: 该半自动数据标注系统能够有效促进现实场景下多目标多摄像头车辆跟踪数据集的构建，从而提升相关模型的性能。

authors_text: Xiaofu Jin,Xiaozhu Hu,Xiaoying Wei,Mingming Fan
title: Synapse: Interactive Guidance by Demonstration with Trial-and-Error Support for Older Adults to Use Smartphone Apps
title_zh: Synapse：通过演示提供交互式指导，并为老年人使用智能手机应用程序提供试错支持
comment: ef:Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies (IMWUT) 6, 3, Article 121 (September 2022), 24 pages
comments: ef:Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies (IMWUT) 6, 3, Article 121 (September 2022), 24 pages
abstract: As smartphones are widely adopted, mobile applications (apps) are emerging to provide critical services such as food delivery and telemedicine. While bring convenience to everyday life, this trend may create barriers for older adults who tend to be less tech-savvy than young people. In-person or screen sharing support is helpful but limited by the help-givers' availability. Video tutorials can be useful but require users to switch contexts between watching the tutorial and performing the corresponding actions in the app, which is cumbersome to do on a mobile phone. Although interactive tutorials have been shown to be promising, none was designed for older adults. Furthermore, the trial-and-error approach has been shown to be beneficial for older adults, but they often lack support to use the approach. Inspired by both interactive tutorials and trial-and-error approach, we designed an app-independent mobile service, \textit{Synapse}, for help-givers to create a multimodal interactive tutorial on a smartphone and for help-receivers (e.g., older adults) to receive interactive guidance with trial-and-error support when they work on the same task. We conducted a user study with 18 older adults who were 60 and over. Our quantitative and qualitative results show that Synapse provided better support than the traditional video approach and enabled participants to feel more confident and motivated. Lastly, we present further design considerations to better support older adults with trial-and-error on smartphones.
summary: As smartphones are widely adopted, mobile applications (apps) are emerging to provide critical services such as food delivery and telemedicine. While bring convenience to everyday life, this trend may create barriers for older adults who tend to be less tech-savvy than young people. In-person or screen sharing support is helpful but limited by the help-givers' availability. Video tutorials can be useful but require users to switch contexts between watching the tutorial and performing the corresponding actions in the app, which is cumbersome to do on a mobile phone. Although interactive tutorials have been shown to be promising, none was designed for older adults. Furthermore, the trial-and-error approach has been shown to be beneficial for older adults, but they often lack support to use the approach. Inspired by both interactive tutorials and trial-and-error approach, we designed an app-independent mobile service, \textit{Synapse}, for help-givers to create a multimodal interactive tutorial on a smartphone and for help-receivers (e.g., older adults) to receive interactive guidance with trial-and-error support when they work on the same task. We conducted a user study with 18 older adults who were 60 and over. Our quantitative and qualitative results show that Synapse provided better support than the traditional video approach and enabled participants to feel more confident and motivated. Lastly, we present further design considerations to better support older adults with trial-and-error on smartphones.
abstract_zh: 随着智能手机的广泛采用，移动应用程序（app）不断涌现，以提供食品配送和远程医疗等关键服务。虽然给日常生活带来了便利，但这种趋势可能会给老年人带来障碍，因为他们对科技的了解往往不如年轻人。面对面或屏幕共享支持很有帮助，但受到提供帮助者的可用性的限制。视频教程可能很有用，但需要用户在观看教程和在应用程序中执行相应操作之间切换上下文，这在手机上执行起来很麻烦。尽管交互式教程已被证明很有前途，但没有一个教程是为老年人设计的。此外，试错法已被证明对老年人有益，但他们往往缺乏使用该方法的支持。受到交互式教程和试错方法的启发，我们设计了一个独立于应用程序的移动服务 \textit{Synapse}，供帮助者在智能手机上创建多模式交互式教程，并让帮助接受者（例如老年人）在处理同一任务时获得带有试错支持的交互式指导。我们对 18 名 60 岁及以上的老年人进行了一项用户研究。我们的定量和定性结果表明，Synapse 提供了比传统视频方法更好的支持，并使参与者感到更加自信和积极主动。最后，我们提出了进一步的设计考虑，以更好地支持老年人在智能手机上进行试错。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Synapse的手机应用独立服务，通过交互式教程和试错支持帮助老年人更有效地使用智能手机应用。
AI.motivation: 智能手机应用的普及对老年人构成使用障碍，传统视频教程和现有交互式教程无法满足其需求，且缺乏对试错学习的支持。
AI.method: 开发了跨应用的Synapse服务，允许帮助者创建多模态交互教程，受助者（如老年人）在使用过程中可获得试错支持，并通过18名60岁以上用户的实验验证效果。
AI.result: 定量和定性结果表明，Synapse相比传统视频方法更有效，显著提升用户信心和学习动机。
AI.conclusion: Synapse通过交互式指导和试错支持有效帮助老年人使用手机应用，未来需进一步优化设计以增强适老化体验。

Although interactive tutorials have been shown to be promising, none was designed for older adults. Furthermore, the trial-and-error approach has been shown to be beneficial for older adults, but they often lack support to use the approach. Inspired by both interactive tutorials and trial-and-error approach, we designed an app-independent mobile service, \textit{Synapse}, for help-givers to create a multimodal interactive tutorial on a smartphone and for help-receivers (e.g., older adults) to receive interactive guidance with trial-and-error support when they work on the same task. We conducted a user study with 18 older adults who were 60 and over. Our quantitative and qualitative results show that Synapse provided better support than the traditional video approach and enabled participants to feel more confident and motivated. Lastly, we present further design considerations to better support older adults with trial-and-error on smartphones. 随着智能手机的广泛采用，移动应用程序（app）不断涌现，以提供食品配送和远程医疗等关键服务。虽然给日常生活带来了便利，但这种趋势可能会给老年人带来障碍，因为他们对科技的了解往往不如年轻人。面对面或屏幕共享支持很有帮助，但受到提供帮助者的可用性的限制。视频教程可能很有用，但需要用户在观看教程和在应用程序中执行相应操作之间切换上下文，这在手机上执行起来很麻烦。尽管交互式教程已被证明很有前途，但没有一个教程是为老年人设计的。此外，试错法已被证明对老年人有益，但他们往往缺乏使用该方法的支持。受到交互式教程和试错方法的启发，我们设计了一个独立于应用程序的移动服务 \textit{Synapse}，供帮助者在智能手机上创建多模式交互式教程，并让帮助接受者（例如老年人）在处理同一任务时获得带有试错支持的交互式指导。我们对 18 名 60 岁及以上的老年人进行了一项用户研究。我们的定量和定性结果表明，Synapse 提供了比传统视频方法更好的支持，并使参与者感到更加自信和积极主动。最后，我们提出了进一步的设计考虑，以更好地支持老年人在智能手机上进行试错。 本文提出了一种名为Synapse的手机应用独立服务，通过交互式教程和试错支持帮助老年人更有效地使用智能手机应用。 Xiaofu Jin,Xiaozhu Hu,Xiaoying Wei,Mingming Fan cs.HC

authors_text: José Morano,Álvaro S. Hervella,Jorge Novo,José Rouco
title: Simultaneous segmentation and classification of the retinal arteries and veins from color fundus images
title_zh: 彩色眼底图像中视网膜动脉和静脉的同步分割和分类
comment: ef:Artificial Intelligence in Medicine, Volume 118, 2021, 102116, ISSN 0933-3657
comments: ef:Artificial Intelligence in Medicine, Volume 118, 2021, 102116, ISSN 0933-3657
abstract: The study of the retinal vasculature is a fundamental stage in the screening and diagnosis of many diseases. A complete retinal vascular analysis requires to segment and classify the blood vessels of the retina into arteries and veins (A/V). Early automatic methods approached these segmentation and classification tasks in two sequential stages. However, currently, these tasks are approached as a joint semantic segmentation task, as the classification results highly depend on the effectiveness of the vessel segmentation. In that regard, we propose a novel approach for the simultaneous segmentation and classification of the retinal A/V from eye fundus images. In particular, we propose a novel method that, unlike previous approaches, and thanks to a novel loss, decomposes the joint task into three segmentation problems targeting arteries, veins and the whole vascular tree. This configuration allows to handle vessel crossings intuitively and directly provides accurate segmentation masks of the different target vascular trees. The provided ablation study on the public Retinal Images vessel Tree Extraction (RITE) dataset demonstrates that the proposed method provides a satisfactory performance, particularly in the segmentation of the different structures. Furthermore, the comparison with the state of the art shows that our method achieves highly competitive results in A/V classification, while significantly improving vascular segmentation. The proposed multi-segmentation method allows to detect more vessels and better segment the different structures, while achieving a competitive classification performance. Also, in these terms, our approach outperforms the approaches of various reference works. Moreover, in contrast with previous approaches, the proposed method allows to directly detect the vessel crossings, as well as preserving the continuity of A/V at these complex locations.

A complete retinal vascular analysis requires to segment and classify the blood vessels of the retina into arteries and veins (A/V). Early automatic methods approached these segmentation and classification tasks in two sequential stages. However, currently, these tasks are approached as a joint semantic segmentation task, as the classification results highly depend on the effectiveness of the vessel segmentation. In that regard, we propose a novel approach for the simultaneous segmentation and classification of the retinal A/V from eye fundus images. In particular, we propose a novel method that, unlike previous approaches, and thanks to a novel loss, decomposes the joint task into three segmentation problems targeting arteries, veins and the whole vascular tree. This configuration allows to handle vessel crossings intuitively and directly provides accurate segmentation masks of the different target vascular trees. The provided ablation study on the public Retinal Images vessel Tree Extraction (RITE) dataset demonstrates that the proposed method provides a satisfactory performance, particularly in the segmentation of the different structures. Furthermore, the comparison with the state of the art shows that our method achieves highly competitive results in A/V classification, while significantly improving vascular segmentation. The proposed multi-segmentation method allows to detect more vessels and better segment the different structures, while achieving a competitive classification performance. Also, in these terms, our approach outperforms the approaches of various reference works. Moreover, in contrast with previous approaches, the proposed method allows to directly detect the vessel crossings, as well as preserving the continuity of A/V at these complex locations.
abstract_zh: 视网膜脉管系统的研究是许多疾病筛查和诊断的基础阶段。完整的视网膜血管分析需要将视网膜血管分割并分类为动脉和静脉 (A/V)。早期的自动方法分两个连续阶段来处理这些分割和分类任务。然而，目前，这些任务被视为联合语义分割任务，因为分类结果高度依赖于血管分割的有效性。在这方面，我们提出了一种从眼底图像中同时分割和分类视网膜 A/V 的新方法。特别是，我们提出了一种新方法，与以前的方法不同，并且由于新的损失，将联合任务分解为针对动脉、静脉和整个血管树的三个分割问题。这种配置允许直观地处理血管交叉，并直接提供不同目标血管树的准确分割掩模。对公共视网膜图像血管树提取（RITE）数据集提供的消融研究表明，所提出的方法提供了令人满意的性能，特别是在不同结构的分割方面。此外，与现有技术的比较表明，我们的方法在 A/V 分类方面取得了极具竞争力的结果，同时显着改善了血管分割。所提出的多分割方法可以检测更多的血管并更好地分割不同的结构，同时实现有竞争力的分类性能。此外，从这些方面来说，我们的方法优于各种参考文献的方法。此外，与以前的方法相比，所提出的方法允许直接检测船只交叉点，并保持这些复杂位置的 A/V 的连续性。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种从彩色眼底图像中同时分割和分类视网膜动脉与静脉的新方法。
AI.motivation: 传统分步处理分割与分类任务的方法存在依赖性问题，而联合任务能提升分类准确性，但需解决血管交叉等复杂结构处理难题。
AI.method: 设计新型损失函数，将联合任务分解为动脉、静脉及全血管树三个独立分割子任务，直接处理血管交叉并保持结构连续性。
AI.result: 在RITE数据集上，分割性能显著提升，分类结果达到同类最佳，尤其在血管交叉区域表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 该多分割方法在保持分类竞争力的同时，实现了更精确的血管分割与交叉区域处理，优于现有主流方法。

authors_text: Philipp Müller,Michael Dietz,Dominik Schiller,Dominike Thomas,Hali Lindsay,Patrick Gebhard,Elisabeth André,Andreas Bulling
title: MultiMediate '22: Backchannel Detection and Agreement Estimation in Group Interactions
title_zh: MultiMediate '22：群体交互中的反向通道检测和一致性估计
comment: ACM Multimedia 2022
comments: ACM Multimedia 2022
abstract: Backchannels, i.e. short interjections of the listener, serve important meta-conversational purposes like signifying attention or indicating agreement. Despite their key role, automatic analysis of backchannels in group interactions has been largely neglected so far. The MultiMediate challenge addresses, for the first time, the tasks of backchannel detection and agreement estimation from backchannels in group conversations. This paper describes the MultiMediate challenge and presents a novel set of annotations consisting of 7234 backchannel instances for the MPIIGroupInteraction dataset. Each backchannel was additionally annotated with the extent by which it expresses agreement towards the current speaker. In addition to a an analysis of the collected annotations, we present baseline results for both challenge tasks.
summary: Backchannels, i.e. short interjections of the listener, serve important meta-conversational purposes like signifying attention or indicating agreement. Despite their key role, automatic analysis of backchannels in group interactions has been largely neglected so far. The MultiMediate challenge addresses, for the first time, the tasks of backchannel detection and agreement estimation from backchannels in group conversations. This paper describes the MultiMediate challenge and presents a novel set of annotations consisting of 7234 backchannel instances for the MPIIGroupInteraction dataset. Each backchannel was additionally annotated with the extent by which it expresses agreement towards the current speaker. In addition to a an analysis of the collected annotations, we present baseline results for both challenge tasks.
abstract_zh: 反向通道，即听众的简短感叹词，具有重要的元对话目的，例如表示注意或表示同意。尽管发挥着关键作用，但群体互动中反向渠道的自动分析迄今为止在很大程度上被忽视了。 MultiMediate 挑战首次解决了群组对话中反向通道检测和协议估计的任务。本文描述了 MultiMediate 挑战，并提出了一组新颖的注释，其中包含 MPIIGroupInteraction 数据集的 7234 个反向通道实例。每个反向通道都额外注释了它表达对当前发言者的同意的程度。除了对收集的注释进行分析之外，我们还提供了两项挑战任务的基线结果。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了MultiMediate挑战赛，首次针对小组对话中的backchannel检测和同意程度估计任务，提供了标注数据集和基线结果。
AI.motivation: Backchannel在对话中具有重要功能，但其自动分析在小组交互中长期被忽视，亟需系统性研究。
AI.method: 基于MPIIGroupInteraction数据集构建包含7234个backchannel实例的标注集，每个实例标注其表达同意的程度，并分析标注特征及提供基线模型。
AI.result: 完成了标注数据的统计分析，并为backchannel检测和同意估计两个任务提供了初步的基线性能指标。
AI.conclusion: 通过构建首个多模态小组对话backchannel标注集，推动了该领域自动分析方法的研究，为后续工作奠定基础。

authors_text: Seongyeop Yang,Byeongkeun Kang,Yeejin Lee
title: Sampling Agnostic Feature Representation for Long-Term Person Re-identification
title_zh: 用于长期人员重新识别的采样不可知特征表示
comment: 11 pages, 7 figures, In IEEE Transactions on Image Processing 2022
comments: 11 pages, 7 figures, In IEEE Transactions on Image Processing 2022
abstract: Person re-identification is a problem of identifying individuals across non-overlapping cameras. Although remarkable progress has been made in the re-identification problem, it is still a challenging problem due to appearance variations of the same person as well as other people of similar appearance. Some prior works solved the issues by separating features of positive samples from features of negative ones. However, the performances of existing models considerably depend on the characteristics and statistics of the samples used for training. Thus, we propose a novel framework named sampling independent robust feature representation network (SirNet) that learns disentangled feature embedding from randomly chosen samples. A carefully designed sampling independent maximum discrepancy loss is introduced to model samples of the same person as a cluster. As a result, the proposed framework can generate additional hard negatives/positives using the learned features, which results in better discriminability from other identities. Extensive experimental results on large-scale benchmark datasets verify that the proposed model is more effective than prior state-of-the-art models.
summary: Person re-identification is a problem of identifying individuals across non-overlapping cameras. Although remarkable progress has been made in the re-identification problem, it is still a challenging problem due to appearance variations of the same person as well as other people of similar appearance. Some prior works solved the issues by separating features of positive samples from features of negative ones. However, the performances of existing models considerably depend on the characteristics and statistics of the samples used for training. Thus, we propose a novel framework named sampling independent robust feature representation network (SirNet) that learns disentangled feature embedding from randomly chosen samples. A carefully designed sampling independent maximum discrepancy loss is introduced to model samples of the same person as a cluster. As a result, the proposed framework can generate additional hard negatives/positives using the learned features, which results in better discriminability from other identities. Extensive experimental results on large-scale benchmark datasets verify that the proposed model is more effective than prior state-of-the-art models.
abstract_zh: 人员重新识别是跨非重叠摄像机识别人员的问题。尽管重新识别问题已经取得了显着的进展，但由于同一个人以及其他相似外表的人的外表变化，它仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。一些先前的工作通过将正样本的特征与负样本的特征分开来解决这个问题。然而，现有模型的性能在很大程度上取决于用于训练的样本的特征和统计数据。因此，我们提出了一种名为采样独立鲁棒特征表示网络（SirNet）的新颖框架，该框架从随机选择的样本中学习解缠结的特征嵌入。引入了精心设计的采样独立最大差异损失来将同一个人的样本样本建模为一个簇。因此，所提出的框架可以使用学习到的特征生成额外的硬否定/肯定，从而更好地区别其他身份。大规模基准数据集上的大量实验结果验证了所提出的模型比先前最先进的模型更有效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种与采样无关的特征表示方法，用于解决长期人员重识别中的外观变化和相似人物干扰问题。
AI.motivation: 现有模型性能高度依赖训练样本的特征和统计特性，难以应对实际场景中复杂的外观变化和相似人物干扰。
AI.method: 设计名为SirNet的框架，通过采样无关最大差异损失学习解耦特征嵌入，并利用学习到的特征生成额外难样本以增强判别性。
AI.result: 在多个大规模基准数据集上的实验验证表明，该方法优于现有最先进模型，具有更强的识别能力。
AI.conclusion: 提出的采样无关特征表示方法有效提升了长期人员重识别的鲁棒性和准确性，为相关研究提供了新思路。

search_text: Sampling Agnostic Feature Representation for Long-Term Person Re-identification 用于长期人员重新识别的采样不可知特征表示 Person re-identification is a problem of identifying individuals across non-overlapping cameras. Although remarkable progress has been made in the re-identification problem, it is still a challenging problem due to appearance variations of the same person as well as other people of similar appearance. Some prior works solved the issues by separating features of positive samples from features of negative ones. However, the performances of existing models considerably depend on the characteristics and statistics of the samples used for training. Thus, we propose a novel framework named sampling independent robust feature representation network (SirNet) that learns disentangled feature embedding from randomly chosen samples. A carefully designed sampling independent maximum discrepancy loss is introduced to model samples of the same person as a cluster. As a result, the proposed framework can generate additional hard negatives/positives using the learned features, which results in better discriminability from other identities. Extensive experimental results on large-scale benchmark datasets verify that the proposed model is more effective than prior state-of-the-art models. 人员重新识别是跨非重叠摄像机识别人员的问题。尽管重新识别问题已经取得了显着的进展，但由于同一个人以及其他相似外表的人的外表变化，它仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。一些先前的工作通过将正样本的特征与负样本的特征分开来解决这个问题。然而，现有模型的性能在很大程度上取决于用于训练的样本的特征和统计数据。因此，我们提出了一种名为采样独立鲁棒特征表示网络（SirNet）的新颖框架，该框架从随机选择的样本中学习解缠结的特征嵌入。引入了精心设计的采样独立最大差异损失来将同一个人的样本样本建模为一个簇。因此，所提出的框架可以使用学习到的特征生成额外的硬否定/肯定，从而更好地区别其他身份。大规模基准数据集上的大量实验结果验证了所提出的模型比先前最先进的模型更有效。 本文提出一种与采样无关的特征表示方法，用于解决长期人员重识别中的外观变化和相似人物干扰问题。 Seongyeop Yang,Byeongkeun Kang,Yeejin Lee cs.CV

authors_text: Mikhail Kiselev
title: A Spiking Neural Network Learning Markov Chain
title_zh: 学习马尔可夫链的尖峰神经网络
abstract: In this paper, the question how spiking neural network (SNN) learns and fixes in its internal structures a model of external world dynamics is explored. This question is important for implementation of the model-based reinforcement learning (RL), the realistic RL regime where the decisions made by SNN and their evaluation in terms of reward/punishment signals may be separated by significant time interval and sequence of intermediate evaluation-neutral world states. In the present work, I formalize world dynamics as a Markov chain with unknown a priori state transition probabilities, which should be learnt by the network. To make this problem formulation more realistic, I solve it in continuous time, so that duration of every state in the Markov chain may be different and is unknown. It is demonstrated how this task can be accomplished by an SNN with specially designed structure and local synaptic plasticity rules. As an example, we show how this network motif works in the simple but non-trivial world where a ball moves inside a square box and bounces from its walls with a random new direction and velocity.
summary: In this paper, the question how spiking neural network (SNN) learns and fixes in its internal structures a model of external world dynamics is explored. This question is important for implementation of the model-based reinforcement learning (RL), the realistic RL regime where the decisions made by SNN and their evaluation in terms of reward/punishment signals may be separated by significant time interval and sequence of intermediate evaluation-neutral world states. In the present work, I formalize world dynamics as a Markov chain with unknown a priori state transition probabilities, which should be learnt by the network. To make this problem formulation more realistic, I solve it in continuous time, so that duration of every state in the Markov chain may be different and is unknown. It is demonstrated how this task can be accomplished by an SNN with specially designed structure and local synaptic plasticity rules. As an example, we show how this network motif works in the simple but non-trivial world where a ball moves inside a square box and bounces from its walls with a random new direction and velocity.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，探讨了尖峰神经网络（SNN）如何学习并在其内部结构中修复外部世界动态模型的问题。这个问题对于基于模型的强化学习 (RL) 的实现非常重要，在现实的 RL 机制中，SNN 做出的决策及其在奖励/惩罚信号方面的评估可能被显着的时间间隔和中间评估中性世界状态的序列分开。在目前的工作中，我将世界动力学形式化为具有未知先验状态转换概率的马尔可夫链，该概率应该由网络学习。为了使这个问题的表述更加现实，我在连续时间内解决它，因此马尔可夫链中每个状态的持续时间可能不同并且是未知的。演示了如何通过具有专门设计的结构和局部突触可塑性规则的 SNN 来完成此任务。作为一个例子，我们展示了这个网络主题如何在简单但不平凡的世界中工作，其中一个球在一个方框内移动，并以随机的新方向和速度从方框的墙壁上弹起。

AI.tldr: 本文研究脉冲神经网络（SNN）如何学习并表征外部世界动态模型，具体为在连续时间框架下学习未知马尔可夫链的状态转移概率。
AI.motivation: 基于模型的强化学习需要SNN在存在时间延迟和中间状态的情况下，独立学习环境动态模型，这对传统离散时间方法提出挑战。
AI.method: 将世界动态形式化为连续时间马尔可夫链，设计具有局部突触可塑性规则的SNN结构，通过网络内部状态与外部环境的交互实现动态学习。
AI.result: 验证了该SNN结构可有效学习马尔可夫链模型，并通过球体在方形盒内运动的实例展示了网络对连续时间状态转移的建模能力。
AI.conclusion: 提出了一种新型SNN架构和学习机制，使神经网络能够直接学习连续时间马尔可夫链模型，为基于模型的强化学习提供了新思路。

authors_text: Thomas Buddenkotte,Lorena Escudero Sanchez,Mireia Crispin-Ortuzar,Ramona Woitek,Cathal McCague,James D. Brenton,Ozan Öktem,Evis Sala,Leonardo Rundo
title: Calibrating Ensembles for Scalable Uncertainty Quantification in Deep Learning-based Medical Segmentation
title_zh: 在基于深度学习的医疗分割中校准集成以实现可扩展的不确定性量化
abstract: Uncertainty quantification in automated image analysis is highly desired in many applications. Typically, machine learning models in classification or segmentation are only developed to provide binary answers; however, quantifying the uncertainty of the models can play a critical role for example in active learning or machine human interaction. Uncertainty quantification is especially difficult when using deep learning-based models, which are the state-of-the-art in many imaging applications. The current uncertainty quantification approaches do not scale well in high-dimensional real-world problems. Scalable solutions often rely on classical techniques, such as dropout, during inference or training ensembles of identical models with different random seeds to obtain a posterior distribution. In this paper, we show that these approaches fail to approximate the classification probability. On the contrary, we propose a scalable and intuitive framework to calibrate ensembles of deep learning models to produce uncertainty quantification measurements that approximate the classification probability. On unseen test data, we demonstrate improved calibration, sensitivity (in two out of three cases) and precision when being compared with the standard approaches. We further motivate the usage of our method in active learning, creating pseudo-labels to learn from unlabeled images and human-machine collaboration.
summary: Uncertainty quantification in automated image analysis is highly desired in many applications. Typically, machine learning models in classification or segmentation are only developed to provide binary answers; however, quantifying the uncertainty of the models can play a critical role for example in active learning or machine human interaction. Uncertainty quantification is especially difficult when using deep learning-based models, which are the state-of-the-art in many imaging applications. The current uncertainty quantification approaches do not scale well in high-dimensional real-world problems. Scalable solutions often rely on classical techniques, such as dropout, during inference or training ensembles of identical models with different random seeds to obtain a posterior distribution. In this paper, we show that these approaches fail to approximate the classification probability. On the contrary, we propose a scalable and intuitive framework to calibrate ensembles of deep learning models to produce uncertainty quantification measurements that approximate the classification probability. On unseen test data, we demonstrate improved calibration, sensitivity (in two out of three cases) and precision when being compared with the standard approaches. We further motivate the usage of our method in active learning, creating pseudo-labels to learn from unlabeled images and human-machine collaboration.
abstract_zh: 在许多应用中，非常需要自动图像分析中的不确定性量化。通常，分类或分割中的机器学习模型仅用于提供二进制答案；然而，量化模型的不确定性可以在主动学习或机器人机交互等领域发挥关键作用。使用基于深度学习的模型时，不确定性量化尤其困难，而深度学习模型是许多成像应用中最先进的模型。当前的不确定性量化方法在高维现实问题中不能很好地扩展。可扩展的解决方案通常依赖于经典技术，例如在使用不同随机种子的相同模型的推理或训练集成过程中使用 dropout 来获得后验分布。在本文中，我们表明这些方法无法近似分类概率。相反，我们提出了一个可扩展且直观的框架来校准深度学习模型的集合，以产生近似分类概率的不确定性量化测量。在看不见的测试数据上，与标准方法相比，我们证明了校准、灵敏度（三分之二）和精度的改进。我们进一步激励我们的方法在主动学习中的使用，创建伪标签来从未标记的图像和人机协作中学习。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可扩展的深度学习模型集成校准框架，用于改进医学图像分割中的不确定性量化。
AI.motivation: 现有不确定性量化方法在高维医学影像任务中扩展性差，无法准确近似分类概率，影响主动学习与人机协作等应用。
AI.method: 通过校准模型集成框架，使不确定性度量更贴近真实分类概率，替代传统dropout或随机种子生成的集成方法。
AI.result: 在测试数据上实现更优的校准效果，灵敏度（2/3案例）和精确度优于基准方法，并验证了主动学习场景下的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为医学分割提供了可靠的不确定性评估，适用于伪标签生成与人机协作，未来可探索更高效的集成策略。

tldr: 本文提出一种可扩展的深度学习模型集成校准框架，用于改进医学图像分割中的不确定性量化。
motivation: 现有不确定性量化方法在高维医学影像任务中扩展性差，无法准确近似分类概率，影响主动学习与人机协作等应用。

result: 在测试数据上实现更优的校准效果，灵敏度（2/3案例）和精确度优于基准方法，并验证了主动学习场景下的有效性。

search_text: Calibrating Ensembles for Scalable Uncertainty Quantification in Deep Learning-based Medical Segmentation 在基于深度学习的医疗分割中校准集成以实现可扩展的不确定性量化 Uncertainty quantification in automated image analysis is highly desired in many applications. Typically, machine learning models in classification or segmentation are only developed to provide binary answers; however, quantifying the uncertainty of the models can play a critical role for example in active learning or machine human interaction. Uncertainty quantification is especially difficult when using deep learning-based models, which are the state-of-the-art in many imaging applications. The current uncertainty quantification approaches do not scale well in high-dimensional real-world problems. Scalable solutions often rely on classical techniques, such as dropout, during inference or training ensembles of identical models with different random seeds to obtain a posterior distribution. In this paper, we show that these approaches fail to approximate the classification probability. On the contrary, we propose a scalable and intuitive framework to calibrate ensembles of deep learning models to produce uncertainty quantification measurements that approximate the classification probability. On unseen test data, we demonstrate improved calibration, sensitivity (in two out of three cases) and precision when being compared with the standard approaches. We further motivate the usage of our method in active learning, creating pseudo-labels to learn from unlabeled images and human-machine collaboration. 在许多应用中，非常需要自动图像分析中的不确定性量化。通常，分类或分割中的机器学习模型仅用于提供二进制答案；然而，量化模型的不确定性可以在主动学习或机器人机交互等领域发挥关键作用。使用基于深度学习的模型时，不确定性量化尤其困难，而深度学习模型是许多成像应用中最先进的模型。当前的不确定性量化方法在高维现实问题中不能很好地扩展。可扩展的解决方案通常依赖于经典技术，例如在使用不同随机种子的相同模型的推理或训练集成过程中使用 dropout 来获得后验分布。在本文中，我们表明这些方法无法近似分类概率。相反，我们提出了一个可扩展且直观的框架来校准深度学习模型的集合，以产生近似分类概率的不确定性量化测量。在看不见的测试数据上，与标准方法相比，我们证明了校准、灵敏度（三分之二）和精度的改进。我们进一步激励我们的方法在主动学习中的使用，创建伪标签来从未标记的图像和人机协作中学习。 本文提出一种可扩展的深度学习模型集成校准框架，用于改进医学图像分割中的不确定性量化。 Thomas Buddenkotte,Lorena Escudero Sanchez,Mireia Crispin-Ortuzar,Ramona Woitek,Cathal McCague,James D. Brenton,Ozan Öktem,Evis Sala,Leonardo Rundo cs.LG,cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Sadia Khanam,Mohammad Reza Chalak Qazani,Subrota Kumar Mondal,H M Dipu Kabir,Abadhan S. Sabyasachi,Houshyar Asadi,Keshav Kumar,Farzin Tabarsinezhad,Shady Mohamed,Abbas Khorsavi,Saeid Nahavandi
title: CoV-TI-Net: Transferred Initialization with Modified End Layer for COVID-19 Diagnosis
title_zh: CoV-TI-Net：用于 COVID-19 诊断的经过修改的末端层的转移初始化
abstract: This paper proposes transferred initialization with modified fully connected layers for COVID-19 diagnosis. Convolutional neural networks (CNN) achieved a remarkable result in image classification. However, training a high-performing model is a very complicated and time-consuming process because of the complexity of image recognition applications. On the other hand, transfer learning is a relatively new learning method that has been employed in many sectors to achieve good performance with fewer computations. In this research, the PyTorch pre-trained models (VGG19\_bn and WideResNet -101) are applied in the MNIST dataset for the first time as initialization and with modified fully connected layers. The employed PyTorch pre-trained models were previously trained in ImageNet. The proposed model is developed and verified in the Kaggle notebook, and it reached the outstanding accuracy of 99.77% without taking a huge computational time during the training process of the network. We also applied the same methodology to the SIIM-FISABIO-RSNA COVID-19 Detection dataset and achieved 80.01% accuracy. In contrast, the previous methods need a huge compactional time during the training process to reach a high-performing model. Codes are available at the following link: github.com/dipuk0506/SpinalNet
summary: This paper proposes transferred initialization with modified fully connected layers for COVID-19 diagnosis. Convolutional neural networks (CNN) achieved a remarkable result in image classification. However, training a high-performing model is a very complicated and time-consuming process because of the complexity of image recognition applications. On the other hand, transfer learning is a relatively new learning method that has been employed in many sectors to achieve good performance with fewer computations. In this research, the PyTorch pre-trained models (VGG19\_bn and WideResNet -101) are applied in the MNIST dataset for the first time as initialization and with modified fully connected layers. The employed PyTorch pre-trained models were previously trained in ImageNet. The proposed model is developed and verified in the Kaggle notebook, and it reached the outstanding accuracy of 99.77% without taking a huge computational time during the training process of the network. We also applied the same methodology to the SIIM-FISABIO-RSNA COVID-19 Detection dataset and achieved 80.01% accuracy. In contrast, the previous methods need a huge compactional time during the training process to reach a high-performing model. Codes are available at the following link: github.com/dipuk0506/SpinalNet
abstract_zh: 本文提出使用修改后的全连接层进行转移初始化来进行 COVID-19 诊断。卷积神经网络（CNN）在图像分类方面取得了显着的成果。然而，由于图像识别应用的复杂性，训练高性能模型是一个非常复杂且耗时的过程。另一方面，迁移学习是一种相对较新的学习方法，已被应用于许多领域，可以通过更少的计算获得良好的性能。在这项研究中，PyTorch 预训练模型（VGG19\_bn 和 WideResNet -101）首次应用于 MNIST 数据集作为初始化并修改了全连接层。使用的 PyTorch 预训练模型之前已在 ImageNet 中进行过训练。所提出的模型在 Kaggle 笔记本中进行开发和验证，在网络训练过程中无需花费大量计算时间即可达到 99.77% 的出色准确率。我们还将相同的方法应用于 SIIM-FISABIO-RSNA COVID-19 检测数据集，并实现了 80.01% 的准确率。相比之下，以前的方法在训练过程中需要大量的压缩时间才能达到高性能模型。代码可从以下链接获取：github.com/dipuk0506/SpinalNet

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于迁移学习和修改全连接层的CoV-TI-Net模型，用于COVID-19诊断。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN训练过程复杂且耗时，而迁移学习能以更少计算量实现高性能。现有方法训练时间长，需优化模型效率。
AI.method: 采用PyTorch预训练模型（VGG19_bn和WideResNet-101），在MNIST数据集上进行迁移初始化并修改全连接层，应用于Kaggle和SIIM-FISABIO-RSNA数据集。
AI.result: 在MNIST数据集上达到99.77%的准确率，在SIIM数据集上达到80.01%的准确率，且训练时间显著缩短。
AI.conclusion: 迁移初始化方法在保持高精度的同时大幅减少训练时间，为COVID-19诊断提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Jianzong Wu,Xiangtai Li,Xia Li,Henghui Ding,Yunhai Tong,Dacheng Tao
title: Towards Robust Referring Image Segmentation
title_zh: 迈向稳健的参考图像分割
comment: update more results
comments: update more results
abstract: Referring Image Segmentation (RIS) is a fundamental vision-language task that outputs object masks based on text descriptions. Many works have achieved considerable progress for RIS, including different fusion method designs. In this work, we explore an essential question, ``What if the text description is wrong or misleading?'' For example, the described objects are not in the image. We term such a sentence as a negative sentence. However, existing solutions for RIS cannot handle such a setting. To this end, we propose a new formulation of RIS, named Robust Referring Image Segmentation (R-RIS). It considers the negative sentence inputs besides the regular positive text inputs. To facilitate this new task, we create three R-RIS datasets by augmenting existing RIS datasets with negative sentences and propose new metrics to evaluate both types of inputs in a unified manner. Furthermore, we propose a new transformer-based model, called RefSegformer, with a token-based vision and language fusion module. Our design can be easily extended to our R-RIS setting by adding extra blank tokens. Our proposed RefSegformer achieves state-of-the-art results on both RIS and R-RIS datasets, establishing a solid baseline for both settings. Our project page is at \url{https://github.com/jianzongwu/robust-ref-seg}.
summary: Referring Image Segmentation (RIS) is a fundamental vision-language task that outputs object masks based on text descriptions. Many works have achieved considerable progress for RIS, including different fusion method designs. In this work, we explore an essential question, ``What if the text description is wrong or misleading?'' For example, the described objects are not in the image. We term such a sentence as a negative sentence. However, existing solutions for RIS cannot handle such a setting. To this end, we propose a new formulation of RIS, named Robust Referring Image Segmentation (R-RIS). It considers the negative sentence inputs besides the regular positive text inputs. To facilitate this new task, we create three R-RIS datasets by augmenting existing RIS datasets with negative sentences and propose new metrics to evaluate both types of inputs in a unified manner. Furthermore, we propose a new transformer-based model, called RefSegformer, with a token-based vision and language fusion module. Our design can be easily extended to our R-RIS setting by adding extra blank tokens. Our proposed RefSegformer achieves state-of-the-art results on both RIS and R-RIS datasets, establishing a solid baseline for both settings. Our project page is at \url{https://github.com/jianzongwu/robust-ref-seg}.
abstract_zh: 参考图像分割（RIS）是一项基本的视觉语言任务，它根据文本描述输出对象掩模。许多工作在RIS方面取得了相当大的进展，包括不同的融合方法设计。在这项工作中，我们探讨了一个基本问题：“如果文本描述错误或具有误导性怎么办？”例如，所描述的物体不在图像中。我们将这样的句子称为否定句。然而，现有的 RIS 解决方案无法处理此类设置。为此，我们提出了一种新的 RIS 公式，称为鲁棒参考图像分割（R-RIS）。除了常规的肯定文本输入之外，它还考虑否定句子输入。为了促进这项新任务，我们通过用否定句扩充现有的 RIS 数据集来创建三个 R-RIS 数据集，并提出新的指标来以统一的方式评估两种类型的输入。此外，我们提出了一种新的基于变压器的模型，称为 RefSegformer，具有基于令牌的视觉和语言融合模块。通过添加额外的空白标记，我们的设计可以轻松扩展到我们的 R-RIS 设置。我们提出的 RefSegformer 在 RIS 和 R-RIS 数据集上都取得了最先进的结果，为这两种设置建立了坚实的基线。我们的项目页面位于 \url{https://github.com/jianzongwu/robust-ref-seg}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种鲁棒的指称图像分割方法（R-RIS），通过引入负样本文本描述并设计新模型来处理误导性文本输入。
AI.motivation: 现有指称图像分割（RIS）方法无法处理文本描述错误或图像中不存在目标的情况，导致分割结果不可靠，需提升模型对负样本的鲁棒性。
AI.method: 提出R-RIS任务框架，构建包含负样本的三个数据集，设计基于Transformer的RefSegformer模型，通过token级视觉-语言融合模块及空白token处理负输入。
AI.result: RefSegformer在RIS和R-RIS数据集上均取得SOTA结果，新指标和数据集为后续研究提供了基准。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入负样本处理机制和新型模型架构，本文为鲁棒指称图像分割任务建立了有效解决方案和研究基础。

search_text: Towards Robust Referring Image Segmentation 迈向稳健的参考图像分割 Referring Image Segmentation (RIS) is a fundamental vision-language task that outputs object masks based on text descriptions. Many works have achieved considerable progress for RIS, including different fusion method designs. In this work, we explore an essential question, ``What if the text description is wrong or misleading?'' For example, the described objects are not in the image. We term such a sentence as a negative sentence. However, existing solutions for RIS cannot handle such a setting. To this end, we propose a new formulation of RIS, named Robust Referring Image Segmentation (R-RIS). It considers the negative sentence inputs besides the regular positive text inputs. To facilitate this new task, we create three R-RIS datasets by augmenting existing RIS datasets with negative sentences and propose new metrics to evaluate both types of inputs in a unified manner. Furthermore, we propose a new transformer-based model, called RefSegformer, with a token-based vision and language fusion module. Our design can be easily extended to our R-RIS setting by adding extra blank tokens. Our proposed RefSegformer achieves state-of-the-art results on both RIS and R-RIS datasets, establishing a solid baseline for both settings. Our project page is at \url{https://github.com/jianzongwu/robust-ref-seg}. 参考图像分割（RIS）是一项基本的视觉语言任务，它根据文本描述输出对象掩模。许多工作在RIS方面取得了相当大的进展，包括不同的融合方法设计。在这项工作中，我们探讨了一个基本问题：“如果文本描述错误或具有误导性怎么办？”例如，所描述的物体不在图像中。我们将这样的句子称为否定句。然而，现有的 RIS 解决方案无法处理此类设置。为此，我们提出了一种新的 RIS 公式，称为鲁棒参考图像分割（R-RIS）。除了常规的肯定文本输入之外，它还考虑否定句子输入。为了促进这项新任务，我们通过用否定句扩充现有的 RIS 数据集来创建三个 R-RIS 数据集，并提出新的指标来以统一的方式评估两种类型的输入。此外，我们提出了一种新的基于变压器的模型，称为 RefSegformer，具有基于令牌的视觉和语言融合模块。通过添加额外的空白标记，我们的设计可以轻松扩展到我们的 R-RIS 设置。我们提出的 RefSegformer 在 RIS 和 R-RIS 数据集上都取得了最先进的结果，为这两种设置建立了坚实的基线。我们的项目页面位于 \url{https://github.com/jianzongwu/robust-ref-seg}。 本文提出了一种鲁棒的指称图像分割方法（R-RIS），通过引入负样本文本描述并设计新模型来处理误导性文本输入。 Jianzong Wu,Xiangtai Li,Xia Li,Henghui Ding,Yunhai Tong,Dacheng Tao cs.CV

authors_text: Emanuele Aiello,Diego Valsesia,Enrico Magli
title: Cross-modal Learning for Image-Guided Point Cloud Shape Completion
title_zh: 用于图像引导点云形状补全的跨模态学习
comment: NeurIPS 2022
comments: NeurIPS 2022
abstract: In this paper we explore the recent topic of point cloud completion, guided by an auxiliary image. We show how it is possible to effectively combine the information from the two modalities in a localized latent space, thus avoiding the need for complex point cloud reconstruction methods from single views used by the state-of-the-art. We also investigate a novel weakly-supervised setting where the auxiliary image provides a supervisory signal to the training process by using a differentiable renderer on the completed point cloud to measure fidelity in the image space. Experiments show significant improvements over state-of-the-art supervised methods for both unimodal and multimodal completion. We also show the effectiveness of the weakly-supervised approach which outperforms a number of supervised methods and is competitive with the latest supervised models only exploiting point cloud information.
summary: In this paper we explore the recent topic of point cloud completion, guided by an auxiliary image. We show how it is possible to effectively combine the information from the two modalities in a localized latent space, thus avoiding the need for complex point cloud reconstruction methods from single views used by the state-of-the-art. We also investigate a novel weakly-supervised setting where the auxiliary image provides a supervisory signal to the training process by using a differentiable renderer on the completed point cloud to measure fidelity in the image space. Experiments show significant improvements over state-of-the-art supervised methods for both unimodal and multimodal completion. We also show the effectiveness of the weakly-supervised approach which outperforms a number of supervised methods and is competitive with the latest supervised models only exploiting point cloud information.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们在辅助图像的指导下探讨了最近的点云补全主题。我们展示了如何在局部潜在空间中有效地组合来自两种模态的信息，从而避免了对最先进的单一视图的复杂点云重建方法的需要。我们还研究了一种新颖的弱监督设置，其中辅助图像通过在完成的点云上使用可微渲染器来测量图像空间的保真度，从而为训练过程提供监督信号。实验表明，单模态和多模态完成方面比最先进的监督方法有显着改进。我们还展示了弱监督方法的有效性，该方法优于许多监督方法，并且与仅利用点云信息的最新监督模型具有竞争力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于跨模态学习的图像引导点云形状补全方法，通过结合图像与点云信息提升补全效果。
AI.motivation: 现有点云补全方法依赖复杂重建技术或单视角信息，难以有效融合多模态数据，且监督方法依赖大量标注数据。
AI.method: 在局部潜在空间中融合图像与点云信息，设计可微分渲染器于弱监督框架下，通过图像空间保真度提供监督信号。
AI.result: 在单模态与多模态补全任务中显著优于现有监督方法，弱监督方法超越多个监督模型并接近仅用点云信息的最新模型。
AI.conclusion: 跨模态融合与弱监督策略有效提升点云补全性能，为多模态数据协同提供新思路。

authors_text: Md Mahfuzur Rahman Siddique,Dong Yang,Yufan He,Daguang Xu,Andriy Myronenko
title: Automated ischemic stroke lesion segmentation from 3D MRI
title_zh: 根据 3D MRI 自动分割缺血性中风病灶
comment: ISLES22 challenge report, MICCAI2022
comments: ISLES22 challenge report, MICCAI2022
abstract: Ischemic Stroke Lesion Segmentation challenge (ISLES 2022) offers a platform for researchers to compare their solutions to 3D segmentation of ischemic stroke regions from 3D MRIs. In this work, we describe our solution to ISLES 2022 segmentation task. We re-sample all images to a common resolution, use two input MRI modalities (DWI and ADC) and train SegResNet semantic segmentation network from MONAI. The final submission is an ensemble of 15 models (from 3 runs of 5-fold cross validation). Our solution (team name NVAUTO) achieves the top place in terms of Dice metric (0.824), and overall rank 2 (based on the combined metric ranking).
summary: Ischemic Stroke Lesion Segmentation challenge (ISLES 2022) offers a platform for researchers to compare their solutions to 3D segmentation of ischemic stroke regions from 3D MRIs. In this work, we describe our solution to ISLES 2022 segmentation task. We re-sample all images to a common resolution, use two input MRI modalities (DWI and ADC) and train SegResNet semantic segmentation network from MONAI. The final submission is an ensemble of 15 models (from 3 runs of 5-fold cross validation). Our solution (team name NVAUTO) achieves the top place in terms of Dice metric (0.824), and overall rank 2 (based on the combined metric ranking).
abstract_zh: 缺血性中风病变分割挑战赛 (ISLES 2022) 为研究人员提供了一个平台，将他们的解决方案与 3D MRI 缺血性中风区域的 3D 分割进行比较。在这项工作中，我们描述了 ISLES 2022 分割任务的解决方案。我们将所有图像重新采样为通用分辨率，使用两种输入 MRI 模式（DWI 和 ADC）并训练 MONAI 的 SegResNet 语义分割网络。最终提交的是 15 个模型的集合（来自 3 次 5 倍交叉验证）。我们的解决方案（团队名称 NVAUTO）在 Dice 指标 (0.824) 方面名列前茅，总体排名第 2（基于综合指标排名）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于3D MRI的缺血性卒中病灶自动分割方法，并在ISLES 2022挑战赛中取得优异成绩。
AI.motivation: 缺血性卒中病灶的精确分割对临床诊断和治疗至关重要，但现有方法在处理3D MRI数据时仍面临挑战。
AI.method: 对图像进行重采样以统一分辨率，结合DWI和ADC两种模态数据，使用MONAI框架训练SegResNet分割网络，并通过15个模型的集成（3次5折交叉验证）提升性能。
AI.result: 在Dice指标上取得0.824的最优成绩，综合排名位列第二。
AI.conclusion: 提出的多模态融合与模型集成方法有效提升了3D卒中病灶分割的准确性，验证了该方案在医学影像分析中的潜力。

authors_text: Dong Zhang,Jinhui Tang,Kwang-Ting Cheng
title: Graph Reasoning Transformer for Image Parsing
title_zh: 用于图像解析的图推理转换器
comment: Accepted in ACM MM2022
comments: Accepted in ACM MM2022
abstract: Capturing the long-range dependencies has empirically proven to be effective on a wide range of computer vision tasks. The progressive advances on this topic have been made through the employment of the transformer framework with the help of the multi-head attention mechanism. However, the attention-based image patch interaction potentially suffers from problems of redundant interactions of intra-class patches and unoriented interactions of inter-class patches. In this paper, we propose a novel Graph Reasoning Transformer (GReaT) for image parsing to enable image patches to interact following a relation reasoning pattern. Specifically, the linearly embedded image patches are first projected into the graph space, where each node represents the implicit visual center for a cluster of image patches and each edge reflects the relation weight between two adjacent nodes. After that, global relation reasoning is performed on this graph accordingly. Finally, all nodes including the relation information are mapped back into the original space for subsequent processes. Compared to the conventional transformer, GReaT has higher interaction efficiency and a more purposeful interaction pattern. Experiments are carried out on the challenging Cityscapes and ADE20K datasets. Results show that GReaT achieves consistent performance gains with slight computational overheads on the state-of-the-art transformer baselines.
summary: Capturing the long-range dependencies has empirically proven to be effective on a wide range of computer vision tasks. The progressive advances on this topic have been made through the employment of the transformer framework with the help of the multi-head attention mechanism. However, the attention-based image patch interaction potentially suffers from problems of redundant interactions of intra-class patches and unoriented interactions of inter-class patches. In this paper, we propose a novel Graph Reasoning Transformer (GReaT) for image parsing to enable image patches to interact following a relation reasoning pattern. Specifically, the linearly embedded image patches are first projected into the graph space, where each node represents the implicit visual center for a cluster of image patches and each edge reflects the relation weight between two adjacent nodes. After that, global relation reasoning is performed on this graph accordingly. Finally, all nodes including the relation information are mapped back into the original space for subsequent processes. Compared to the conventional transformer, GReaT has higher interaction efficiency and a more purposeful interaction pattern. Experiments are carried out on the challenging Cityscapes and ADE20K datasets. Results show that GReaT achieves consistent performance gains with slight computational overheads on the state-of-the-art transformer baselines.
abstract_zh: 经验证明，捕获远程依赖性对于广泛的计算机视觉任务是有效的。在多头注意力机制的帮助下，通过使用 Transformer 框架，在这个主题上取得了进步。然而，基于注意力的图像块交互可能会遇到类内块的冗余交互和类间块的无向交互的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于图像解析的新型图形推理转换器（GReaT），以使图像块能够按照关系推理模式进行交互。具体来说，线性嵌入的图像块首先被投影到图空间中，其中每个节点代表图像块簇的隐式视觉中心，并且每条边反映两个相邻节点之间的关系权重。之后，对该图进行相应的全局关系推理。最后，将包含关系信息的所有节点映射回原始空间，以供后续处理。与传统的 Transformer 相比，GReaT 具有更高的交互效率和更有目的的交互模式。在具有挑战性的 Cityscapes 和 ADE20K 数据集上进行了实验。结果表明，GReaT 在最先进的 Transformer 基线上以微小的计算开销实现了一致的性能增益。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GReaT的图推理Transformer模型，用于改进图像解析任务中的长距离依赖捕捉问题。
AI.motivation: 传统基于注意力机制的Transformer在处理图像块交互时存在类内冗余交互和类间无向交互的问题，影响解析效果。
AI.method: 将线性嵌入的图像块投影到图空间，构建以视觉中心为节点、关系权重为边的图结构，通过全局关系推理后映射回原始空间。
AI.result: 在Cityscapes和ADE20K数据集上，GReaT在保持计算开销轻微增加的前提下，相比先进Transformer基线实现了性能提升。
AI.conclusion: GReaT通过图推理机制实现了更高效、更有针对性的图像块交互模式，为图像解析任务提供了新思路。

ai_content.conclusion: GReaT通过图推理机制实现了更高效、更有针对性的图像块交互模式，为图像解析任务提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GReaT的图推理Transformer模型，用于改进图像解析任务中的长距离依赖捕捉问题。
motivation: 传统基于注意力机制的Transformer在处理图像块交互时存在类内冗余交互和类间无向交互的问题，影响解析效果。
method: 将线性嵌入的图像块投影到图空间，构建以视觉中心为节点、关系权重为边的图结构，通过全局关系推理后映射回原始空间。
result: 在Cityscapes和ADE20K数据集上，GReaT在保持计算开销轻微增加的前提下，相比先进Transformer基线实现了性能提升。
conclusion: GReaT通过图推理机制实现了更高效、更有针对性的图像块交互模式，为图像解析任务提供了新思路。
search_text: Graph Reasoning Transformer for Image Parsing 用于图像解析的图推理转换器 Capturing the long-range dependencies has empirically proven to be effective on a wide range of computer vision tasks. The progressive advances on this topic have been made through the employment of the transformer framework with the help of the multi-head attention mechanism. However, the attention-based image patch interaction potentially suffers from problems of redundant interactions of intra-class patches and unoriented interactions of inter-class patches. In this paper, we propose a novel Graph Reasoning Transformer (GReaT) for image parsing to enable image patches to interact following a relation reasoning pattern. Specifically, the linearly embedded image patches are first projected into the graph space, where each node represents the implicit visual center for a cluster of image patches and each edge reflects the relation weight between two adjacent nodes. After that, global relation reasoning is performed on this graph accordingly. Finally, all nodes including the relation information are mapped back into the original space for subsequent processes. Compared to the conventional transformer, GReaT has higher interaction efficiency and a more purposeful interaction pattern. Experiments are carried out on the challenging Cityscapes and ADE20K datasets. Results show that GReaT achieves consistent performance gains with slight computational overheads on the state-of-the-art transformer baselines. 经验证明，捕获远程依赖性对于广泛的计算机视觉任务是有效的。在多头注意力机制的帮助下，通过使用 Transformer 框架，在这个主题上取得了进步。然而，基于注意力的图像块交互可能会遇到类内块的冗余交互和类间块的无向交互的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于图像解析的新型图形推理转换器（GReaT），以使图像块能够按照关系推理模式进行交互。具体来说，线性嵌入的图像块首先被投影到图空间中，其中每个节点代表图像块簇的隐式视觉中心，并且每条边反映两个相邻节点之间的关系权重。之后，对该图进行相应的全局关系推理。最后，将包含关系信息的所有节点映射回原始空间，以供后续处理。与传统的 Transformer 相比，GReaT 具有更高的交互效率和更有目的的交互模式。在具有挑战性的 Cityscapes 和 ADE20K 数据集上进行了实验。结果表明，GReaT 在最先进的 Transformer 基线上以微小的计算开销实现了一致的性能增益。 本文提出了一种名为GReaT的图推理Transformer模型，用于改进图像解析任务中的长距离依赖捕捉问题。 Dong Zhang,Jinhui Tang,Kwang-Ting Cheng cs.CV

authors_text: Peter Belcák,Ard Kastrati,Flavio Schenker,Roger Wattenhofer
title: FACT: Learning Governing Abstractions Behind Integer Sequences
title_zh: 事实：学习管理整数序列背后的抽象
comment: Accepted to the 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022) Track on Datasets and Benchmarks. 37 pages
comments: Accepted to the 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022) Track on Datasets and Benchmarks. 37 pages
abstract: Integer sequences are of central importance to the modeling of concepts admitting complete finitary descriptions. We introduce a novel view on the learning of such concepts and lay down a set of benchmarking tasks aimed at conceptual understanding by machine learning models. These tasks indirectly assess model ability to abstract, and challenge them to reason both interpolatively and extrapolatively from the knowledge gained by observing representative examples. To further aid research in knowledge representation and reasoning, we present FACT, the Finitary Abstraction Comprehension Toolkit. The toolkit surrounds a large dataset of integer sequences comprising both organic and synthetic entries, a library for data pre-processing and generation, a set of model performance evaluation tools, and a collection of baseline model implementations, enabling the making of the future advancements with ease.
summary: Integer sequences are of central importance to the modeling of concepts admitting complete finitary descriptions. We introduce a novel view on the learning of such concepts and lay down a set of benchmarking tasks aimed at conceptual understanding by machine learning models. These tasks indirectly assess model ability to abstract, and challenge them to reason both interpolatively and extrapolatively from the knowledge gained by observing representative examples. To further aid research in knowledge representation and reasoning, we present FACT, the Finitary Abstraction Comprehension Toolkit. The toolkit surrounds a large dataset of integer sequences comprising both organic and synthetic entries, a library for data pre-processing and generation, a set of model performance evaluation tools, and a collection of baseline model implementations, enabling the making of the future advancements with ease.
abstract_zh: 整数序列对于接受完整有限描述的概念建模至关重要。我们引入了关于学习此类概念的新颖观点，并制定了一组旨在通过机器学习模型进行概念理解的基准测试任务。这些任务间接评估模型的抽象能力，并挑战他们根据通过观察代表性示例获得的知识进行插值和外推推理。为了进一步帮助知识表示和推理的研究，我们推出了 FACT（有限抽象理解工具包）。该工具包围绕一个包含有机和合成条目的大型整数序列数据集、一个用于数据预处理和生成的库、一组模型性能评估工具以及一组基线模型实现，使未来的进步能够轻松实现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FACT工具包，用于评估机器学习模型对整数序列背后抽象概念的理解与推理能力。
AI.motivation: 整数序列在有限描述建模中具有核心地位，但现有模型在抽象概括和推理能力上存在不足，需建立基准任务推动研究。
AI.method: 开发FACT工具包，包含整数序列数据集、预处理库、评估工具及基线模型，支持插值/外推推理任务。
AI.result: 构建了涵盖有机与合成序列的大型数据集，提供标准化评估框架，为后续研究奠定基础。
AI.conclusion: FACT工具包通过系统化资源和评估手段，推动知识表示与推理领域在整数序列抽象学习方向的研究进展。

authors_text: Finn Rietz,Erik Schaffernicht,Todor Stoyanov,Johannes A. Stork
title: Towards Task-Prioritized Policy Composition
title_zh: 迈向任务优先的政策制定
abstract: Combining learned policies in a prioritized, ordered manner is desirable because it allows for modular design and facilitates data reuse through knowledge transfer. In control theory, prioritized composition is realized by null-space control, where low-priority control actions are projected into the null-space of high-priority control actions. Such a method is currently unavailable for Reinforcement Learning. We propose a novel, task-prioritized composition framework for Reinforcement Learning, which involves a novel concept: The indifferent-space of Reinforcement Learning policies. Our framework has the potential to facilitate knowledge transfer and modular design while greatly increasing data efficiency and data reuse for Reinforcement Learning agents. Further, our approach can ensure high-priority constraint satisfaction, which makes it promising for learning in safety-critical domains like robotics. Unlike null-space control, our approach allows learning globally optimal policies for the compound task by online learning in the indifference-space of higher-level policies after initial compound policy construction.
summary: Combining learned policies in a prioritized, ordered manner is desirable because it allows for modular design and facilitates data reuse through knowledge transfer. In control theory, prioritized composition is realized by null-space control, where low-priority control actions are projected into the null-space of high-priority control actions. Such a method is currently unavailable for Reinforcement Learning. We propose a novel, task-prioritized composition framework for Reinforcement Learning, which involves a novel concept: The indifferent-space of Reinforcement Learning policies. Our framework has the potential to facilitate knowledge transfer and modular design while greatly increasing data efficiency and data reuse for Reinforcement Learning agents. Further, our approach can ensure high-priority constraint satisfaction, which makes it promising for learning in safety-critical domains like robotics. Unlike null-space control, our approach allows learning globally optimal policies for the compound task by online learning in the indifference-space of higher-level policies after initial compound policy construction.
abstract_zh: 以优先、有序的方式组合学习的策略是可取的，因为它允许模块化设计并通过知识转移促进数据重用。在控制理论中，优先级组合是通过零空间控制来实现的，其中低优先级控制动作被投影到高优先级控制动作的零空间中。这种方法目前无法用于强化学习。我们提出了一种新颖的、任务优先的强化学习组合框架，其中涉及一个新颖的概念：强化学习策略的冷漠空间。我们的框架有潜力促进知识转移和模块化设计，同时大大提高强化学习代理的数据效率和数据重用。此外，我们的方法可以确保高优先级的约束满足，这使得它有望在机器人等安全关键领域进行学习。与零空间控制不同，我们的方法允许在初始复合策略构建后通过在更高级别策略的冷漠空间中在线学习来学习复合任务的全局最优策略。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于强化学习的任务优先策略组合框架，通过引入‘无差别空间’概念，解决现有方法在模块化设计和数据重用方面的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习方法缺乏类似控制理论中零空间控制的任务优先级组合机制，难以实现模块化设计、知识迁移及安全关键场景下的高优先级约束满足。
AI.method: 提出任务优先策略组合框架，定义强化学习策略的‘无差别空间’，通过在线学习在高阶策略的无差别空间中优化复合任务策略，确保高优先级约束满足。
AI.result: 框架可提升数据效率与重用性，支持模块化设计，并保证高优先级约束满足，适用于机器人等安全关键领域，实现复合任务的全局最优策略学习。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为强化学习提供了新的策略组合范式，在兼顾模块化与知识迁移的同时，增强了安全关键场景下的可靠性，具有重要应用价值。

authors_text: Joscha Grüger,Tobias Geyer,Martin Kuhn,Stefan Braun,Ralph Bergmann
title: Declarative Guideline Conformance Checking of Clinical Treatments: A Case Study
title_zh: 临床治疗声明性指南一致性检查：案例研究
abstract: Conformance checking is a process mining technique that allows verifying the conformance of process instances to a given model. Thus, this technique is predestined to be used in the medical context for the comparison of treatment cases with clinical guidelines. However, medical processes are highly variable, highly dynamic, and complex. This makes the use of imperative conformance checking approaches in the medical domain difficult. Studies show that declarative approaches can better address these characteristics. However, none of the approaches has yet gained practical acceptance. Another challenge are alignments, which usually do not add any value from a medical point of view. For this reason, we investigate in a case study the usability of the HL7 standard Arden Syntax for declarative, rule-based conformance checking and the use of manually modeled alignments. Using the approach, it was possible to check the conformance of treatment cases and create medically meaningful alignments for large parts of a medical guideline.
summary: Conformance checking is a process mining technique that allows verifying the conformance of process instances to a given model. Thus, this technique is predestined to be used in the medical context for the comparison of treatment cases with clinical guidelines. However, medical processes are highly variable, highly dynamic, and complex. This makes the use of imperative conformance checking approaches in the medical domain difficult. Studies show that declarative approaches can better address these characteristics. However, none of the approaches has yet gained practical acceptance. Another challenge are alignments, which usually do not add any value from a medical point of view. For this reason, we investigate in a case study the usability of the HL7 standard Arden Syntax for declarative, rule-based conformance checking and the use of manually modeled alignments. Using the approach, it was possible to check the conformance of treatment cases and create medically meaningful alignments for large parts of a medical guideline.
abstract_zh: 一致性检查是一种流程挖掘技术，允许验证流程实例与给定模型的一致性。因此，这项技术注定要在医学领域使用，用于将治疗案例与临床指南进行比较。然而，医疗过程是高度可变、高度动态且复杂的。这使得在医疗领域使用强制一致性检查方法变得困难。研究表明，声明式方法可以更好地解决这些特征。然而，这些方法尚未获得实际接受。另一个挑战是对齐，从医学角度来看，对齐通常不会增加任何价值。因此，我们在案例研究中调查了 HL7 标准 Arden 语法在声明性、基于规则的一致性检查以及手动建模对齐的使用方面的可用性。使用该方法，可以检查治疗案例的一致性，并为医疗指南的大部分内容创建具有医学意义的对齐。

AI.tldr: 本文通过案例研究探讨使用声明性方法（HL7 Arden语法）进行临床治疗指南符合性检查的可行性，并评估手动建模对齐的实用性。
AI.motivation: 传统命令式符合性检查难以应对医疗流程的复杂性与动态性，而声明式方法虽具潜力却缺乏实际应用验证，且现有对齐方式缺乏医学意义。
AI.method: 采用HL7标准的Arden语法构建声明性规则模型，结合手动建模的对齐方式，对临床治疗案例与指南进行符合性验证。
AI.result: 成功实现治疗案例的符合性检查，并为医疗指南大部分内容生成具有医学意义的对齐结果。
AI.conclusion: 声明性方法（如Arden语法）可有效支持医疗指南符合性检查，手动对齐能增强结果的医学价值，未来需进一步推广与验证。

search_text: Declarative Guideline Conformance Checking of Clinical Treatments: A Case Study 临床治疗声明性指南一致性检查：案例研究 Conformance checking is a process mining technique that allows verifying the conformance of process instances to a given model. Thus, this technique is predestined to be used in the medical context for the comparison of treatment cases with clinical guidelines. However, medical processes are highly variable, highly dynamic, and complex. This makes the use of imperative conformance checking approaches in the medical domain difficult. Studies show that declarative approaches can better address these characteristics. However, none of the approaches has yet gained practical acceptance. Another challenge are alignments, which usually do not add any value from a medical point of view. For this reason, we investigate in a case study the usability of the HL7 standard Arden Syntax for declarative, rule-based conformance checking and the use of manually modeled alignments. Using the approach, it was possible to check the conformance of treatment cases and create medically meaningful alignments for large parts of a medical guideline. 一致性检查是一种流程挖掘技术，允许验证流程实例与给定模型的一致性。因此，这项技术注定要在医学领域使用，用于将治疗案例与临床指南进行比较。然而，医疗过程是高度可变、高度动态且复杂的。这使得在医疗领域使用强制一致性检查方法变得困难。研究表明，声明式方法可以更好地解决这些特征。然而，这些方法尚未获得实际接受。另一个挑战是对齐，从医学角度来看，对齐通常不会增加任何价值。因此，我们在案例研究中调查了 HL7 标准 Arden 语法在声明性、基于规则的一致性检查以及手动建模对齐的使用方面的可用性。使用该方法，可以检查治疗案例的一致性，并为医疗指南的大部分内容创建具有医学意义的对齐。 本文通过案例研究探讨使用声明性方法（HL7 Arden语法）进行临床治疗指南符合性检查的可行性，并评估手动建模对齐的实用性。 Joscha Grüger,Tobias Geyer,Martin Kuhn,Stefan Braun,Ralph Bergmann cs.AI

authors_text: Michael Tanzer,Sea Hee Yook,Guang Yang,Daniel Rueckert,Sonia Nielles-Vallespin
title: Review of data types and model dimensionality for cardiac DTI SMS-related artefact removal
title_zh: 审查心脏 DTI SMS 相关伪影去除的数据类型和模型维度
comment: 11 pages, 3 tables, 1 figure. To be published at the STACOM workshop, MICCAI 2022
comments: 11 pages, 3 tables, 1 figure. To be published at the STACOM workshop, MICCAI 2022
abstract: As diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) gains popularity in cardiac imaging due to its unique ability to non-invasively assess the cardiac microstructure, deep learning-based Artificial Intelligence is becoming a crucial tool in mitigating some of its drawbacks, such as the long scan times. As it often happens in fast-paced research environments, a lot of emphasis has been put on showing the capability of deep learning while often not enough time has been spent investigating what input and architectural properties would benefit cardiac DTI acceleration the most. In this work, we compare the effect of several input types (magnitude images vs complex images), multiple dimensionalities (2D vs 3D operations), and multiple input types (single slice vs multi-slice) on the performance of a model trained to remove artefacts caused by a simultaneous multi-slice (SMS) acquisition. Despite our initial intuition, our experiments show that, for a fixed number of parameters, simpler 2D real-valued models outperform their more advanced 3D or complex counterparts. The best performance is although obtained by a real-valued model trained using both the magnitude and phase components of the acquired data. We believe this behaviour to be due to real-valued models making better use of the lower number of parameters, and to 3D models not being able to exploit the spatial information because of the low SMS acceleration factor used in our experiments.
summary: As diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) gains popularity in cardiac imaging due to its unique ability to non-invasively assess the cardiac microstructure, deep learning-based Artificial Intelligence is becoming a crucial tool in mitigating some of its drawbacks, such as the long scan times. As it often happens in fast-paced research environments, a lot of emphasis has been put on showing the capability of deep learning while often not enough time has been spent investigating what input and architectural properties would benefit cardiac DTI acceleration the most. In this work, we compare the effect of several input types (magnitude images vs complex images), multiple dimensionalities (2D vs 3D operations), and multiple input types (single slice vs multi-slice) on the performance of a model trained to remove artefacts caused by a simultaneous multi-slice (SMS) acquisition. Despite our initial intuition, our experiments show that, for a fixed number of parameters, simpler 2D real-valued models outperform their more advanced 3D or complex counterparts. The best performance is although obtained by a real-valued model trained using both the magnitude and phase components of the acquired data. We believe this behaviour to be due to real-valued models making better use of the lower number of parameters, and to 3D models not being able to exploit the spatial information because of the low SMS acceleration factor used in our experiments.
abstract_zh: 随着扩散张量成像（DTI）因其独特的非侵入性评估心脏微观结构的能力而在心脏成像中得到普及，基于深度学习的人工智能正在成为缓解其一些缺点（例如扫描时间长）的关键工具。由于这种情况经常发生在快节奏的研究环境中，因此人们非常重视展示深度学习的能力，但往往没有足够的时间来研究哪些输入和架构特性最有利于心脏 DTI 加速。在这项工作中，我们比较了几种输入类型（幅度图像与复杂图像）、多维度（2D 与 3D 操作）和多种输入类型（单切片与多切片）对经过训练以消除同时多切片 (SMS) 采集引起的伪影的模型性能的影响。尽管我们最初的直觉是这样，但我们的实验表明，对于固定数量的参数，更简单的 2D 实值模型优于更先进的 3D 或复杂模型。最佳性能是通过使用所采集数据的幅度和相位分量训练的实值模型获得的。我们认为这种行为是由于实值模型更好地利用了较少数量的参数，并且由于我们实验中使用的 SMS 加速因子较低，3D 模型无法利用空间信息。

AI.tldr: 本论文比较了不同输入类型和模型维度对心脏DTI SMS伪影去除模型性能的影响。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度学习在加速心脏DTI中展现潜力，但现有研究较少系统探讨输入数据类型和模型结构对性能的影响，需填补这一研究空白。
AI.method: 通过对比实验分析了输入类型（幅度/复数图像）、模型维度（2D/3D）及输入切片数（单/多切片）对模型性能的影响，固定参数数量进行公平比较。
AI.result: 固定参数下，2D实值模型优于3D或复数模型，而结合幅度与相位信息的实值模型表现最佳，3D模型因低加速因子未能有效利用空间信息。
AI.conclusion: 实值模型更高效利用参数，3D模型在低加速因子下受限；未来需进一步探索参数分配与空间信息利用策略。

authors_text: Pan Lu,Swaroop Mishra,Tony Xia,Liang Qiu,Kai-Wei Chang,Song-Chun Zhu,Oyvind Tafjord,Peter Clark,Ashwin Kalyan
title: Learn to Explain: Multimodal Reasoning via Thought Chains for Science Question Answering
title_zh: 学会解释：通过思维链进行多模态推理以进行科学问答
comment: Accepted to NeurIPS 2022. 22 pages, 17 figures, 9 tables. Project: https://scienceqa.github.io
comments: Accepted to NeurIPS 2022. 22 pages, 17 figures, 9 tables. Project: https://scienceqa.github.io
abstract: When answering a question, humans utilize the information available across different modalities to synthesize a consistent and complete chain of thought (CoT). This process is normally a black box in the case of deep learning models like large-scale language models. Recently, science question benchmarks have been used to diagnose the multi-hop reasoning ability and interpretability of an AI system. However, existing datasets fail to provide annotations for the answers, or are restricted to the textual-only modality, small scales, and limited domain diversity. To this end, we present Science Question Answering (ScienceQA), a new benchmark that consists of ~21k multimodal multiple choice questions with a diverse set of science topics and annotations of their answers with corresponding lectures and explanations. We further design language models to learn to generate lectures and explanations as the chain of thought (CoT) to mimic the multi-hop reasoning process when answering ScienceQA questions. ScienceQA demonstrates the utility of CoT in language models, as CoT improves the question answering performance by 1.20% in few-shot GPT-3 and 3.99% in fine-tuned UnifiedQA. We also explore the upper bound for models to leverage explanations by feeding those in the input; we observe that it improves the few-shot performance of GPT-3 by 18.96%. Our analysis further shows that language models, similar to humans, benefit from explanations to learn from fewer data and achieve the same performance with just 40% of the data. The data and code are available at https://scienceqa.github.io.
summary: When answering a question, humans utilize the information available across different modalities to synthesize a consistent and complete chain of thought (CoT). This process is normally a black box in the case of deep learning models like large-scale language models. Recently, science question benchmarks have been used to diagnose the multi-hop reasoning ability and interpretability of an AI system. However, existing datasets fail to provide annotations for the answers, or are restricted to the textual-only modality, small scales, and limited domain diversity. To this end, we present Science Question Answering (ScienceQA), a new benchmark that consists of ~21k multimodal multiple choice questions with a diverse set of science topics and annotations of their answers with corresponding lectures and explanations. We further design language models to learn to generate lectures and explanations as the chain of thought (CoT) to mimic the multi-hop reasoning process when answering ScienceQA questions. ScienceQA demonstrates the utility of CoT in language models, as CoT improves the question answering performance by 1.20% in few-shot GPT-3 and 3.99% in fine-tuned UnifiedQA. We also explore the upper bound for models to leverage explanations by feeding those in the input; we observe that it improves the few-shot performance of GPT-3 by 18.96%. Our analysis further shows that language models, similar to humans, benefit from explanations to learn from fewer data and achieve the same performance with just 40% of the data. The data and code are available at https://scienceqa.github.io.
abstract_zh: 在回答问题时，人类利用不同方式提供的信息来综合一致且完整的思维链 (CoT)。对于大规模语言模型等深度学习模型来说，这个过程通常是一个黑匣子。最近，科学问题基准已被用来诊断人工智能系统的多跳推理能力和可解释性。然而，现有数据集无法提供答案的注释，或者仅限于纯文本模式、小规模和有限的领域多样性。为此，我们提出了科学问答 (ScienceQA)，这是一个新的基准，由约 21,000 个多模式多项选择问题组成，其中包含一组不同的科学主题及其答案注释以及相应的讲座和解释。我们进一步设计语言模型来学习生成讲座和解释作为思想链（CoT），以模仿回答 ScienceQA 问题时的多跳推理过程。 ScienceQA 展示了 CoT 在语言模型中的实用性，因为 CoT 在少样本 GPT-3 中将问题回答性能提高了 1.20%，在微调的 UnifiedQA 中将问题回答性能提高了 3.99%。我们还探索模型的上限，通过在输入中提供解释来利用解释；我们观察到它将 GPT-3 的少样本性能提高了 18.96%。我们的分析进一步表明，与人类类似，语言模型受益于解释，可以从更少的数据中学习，并仅用 40% 的数据即可实现相同的性能。数据和代码可在 https://scienceqa.github.io 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为ScienceQA的多模态科学问答基准测试，并设计语言模型通过生成思维链（CoT）来模拟多跳推理过程，以提升科学问题回答的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有科学问答数据集缺乏多模态注释，且规模有限、领域多样性不足，难以有效评估AI系统的多跳推理能力和可解释性。
AI.method: 构建包含约21,000个多模态选择题的ScienceQA基准，提供答案注释及对应讲座和解释；设计语言模型学习生成CoT作为解释，模拟人类推理过程。
AI.result: CoT方法使GPT-3在少样本设置下性能提升1.20%，微调的UnifiedQA提升3.99%；输入解释可使GPT-3少样本性能提升18.96%；模型仅需40%数据即可达到同等效果。
AI.conclusion: 语言模型通过学习生成解释（CoT）可有效提升科学问答性能，且与人类类似，解释能帮助模型以更少数据达到更好效果。
